### sooperb — 2014-03-31T14:30:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-feet-and-i.10398/post-119041>

Audrey, are your feet swollen all the time or do they go down overnight? Swollen feet have a few causes, heart problems (probably unlikely in your case) or vascular issues. I currently have some swelling in my right foot. I'm pretty convinced that it's to do with a developing varicose vein in my right leg, all the signs are there, bumps under the skin, a cluster of tiny veins on my ankle and some pain behind my knee. As it isn't causing problems I haven't bothered about it until the foot started swelling so I guess it's diy vein stripping (I'd have to be very drunk lol) or going to see a doctor or waiting until it's a problem because that's what they will probably tell me. It's apparently considered a cosmetic issue by the NHS so in some areas they don't do anything!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-feet-and-i.10398/post-119295>

> [Tanya said:](/goto/post?id=119290)
>
> Oh, but you need shoes, and especially good looking shoes, if you ever want to be able to move out of your office cubicle

Actually, my office cubicle is one of the only places I wear shoes ... and I use "wear" loosely here.. since I am really just standing in socks rolling my feet on a lacross ball while my shoes are by my feet ready for if I have to venture to the bathroom, or otherwise further than I can get away with in just socks.

If I'm outside (and not immediately going into a store, etc), I'm barefoot! I've been doing this for 6+ months now, phased the barefoot walking in slowly and now do it daily on all my walks.

### cantweight — 2014-04-02T00:43:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-feet-and-i.10398/post-119305>

Audrey, any chance you are sitting for a long time in a different chair or place then you normally do? I had been sitting on our hard kitchen chairs doing my school work online for weeks; developed edema in my lower legs and feet, worse on the right side than the left. Along with the edema my left knee starting killing me, the pain was so bad it was waking me at night but it came and went so i figured nerve. Anyway....my daughter covered the kitchen table for days with a science project and I had to study elsewhere...within a day or 2 the edema went down and the knee pain went with it.

Thought maybe you are sitting somewhere different when on your laptop than maybe you did on your ipad?

Going to get a big yoga ball to sit on!

### nonchalant — 2014-04-02T06:04:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-feet-and-i.10398/post-119360>

I second Tanya's nice ice advice.
When I had problems with my feet, I would partially fill 2 liter bottles with water and freeze, and then hold my feet on them for a while. (This was before I knew of CT.)
Worked wonders for the pain, and the cold was the only thing that ever gave me lasting improvement in my feet.

### Inger — 2014-04-04T08:45:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-feet-and-i.10398/post-119728>

Yay about the labs Audrey

I wonder if it helps hammer toes to walk in naturally bumpy terrain barefoot, like in the woods or on a stony shore? I think I have read somewhere foot gymnastics helps for hammer toes too but I am not sure.. would not hurt to try?

> n the earlier stages of hammer toe, when the toes can still be manually straightened, then conservative treatment is appropriate. This means wearing shoes which are a half size bigger than normal and which are not narrow around the toes. Exercises to stretch the toes out and strengthen the muscles under the foot which balances the tightness of the top tendons are important. Padding or corn plasters can be used to ease the discomfort of any associated corns and calluses.

[http://www.sportsinjuryclinic.net/sport-injuries/foot-heel-pain/hammer-toe-1](http://www.sportsinjuryclinic.net/sport-injuries/foot-heel-pain/hammer-toe-1)

[http://www.sportsinjuryclinic.net/sport-injuries/foot-heel-pain/hammer-toe-1](http://www.sportsinjuryclinic.net/sport-injuries/foot-heel-pain/hammer-toe-1)

### Inger — 2014-04-04T08:45:58+0300

[](http://www.sportsinjuryclinic.net/sport-injuries/foot-heel-pain/hammer-toe-1)

> [](http://www.sportsinjuryclinic.net/sport-injuries/foot-heel-pain/hammer-toe-1)
>
> [
>
> If you notice the beginning signs of hammertoe, you may be able to prevent the tendons from tightening by wearing toe-friendly shoes, by flattening your toes regularly, and by soaking your feet every day in warm water, then stretching your toes and ankles by pointing your toes.
>
> Foot exercises also can help to maintain or restore the flexibility of the tendons. One simple exercise is to place a small towel on the floor and then pick it up using only your toes. You also can grasp at carpet with your toes or curl your toes up and down repeatedly.
>
> ](http://www.sportsinjuryclinic.net/sport-injuries/foot-heel-pain/hammer-toe-1)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-feet-and-i.10398/post-246595>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=119041)
>
> Audrey, are your feet swollen all the time or do they go down overnight? Swollen feet have a few causes, heart problems (probably unlikely in your case) or vascular issues. I currently have some swelling in my right foot. I'm pretty convinced that it's to do with a developing varicose vein in my right leg, all the signs are there, bumps under the skin, a cluster of tiny veins on my ankle and some pain behind my knee. As it isn't causing problems I haven't bothered about it until the foot started swelling so I guess it's diy vein stripping (I'd have to be very drunk lol) or going to see a doctor or waiting until it's a problem because that's what they will probably tell me. It's apparently considered a cosmetic issue by the NHS so in some areas they don't do anything!

I have a couple of varicose veins too but mostly spider veins. I have a family history of deep vein thrombosis so not sure if that's relevant. My sister-in-law works in a doctor's office and she has varicose vein treatment all the time (twice a year). She tells me that we have too many veins and we should prune them like a tree but this doesn't seem logical to me. Has anyone here read of any harm that comes from varicose vein removal or laser treatment? @ [sooperb](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/sooperb.8412/) [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

Barefoot walking is great advice.
It worked for me.
But I had a pain dancing.
For mere $500 for orthotics, insurance paid for doctors visit, and I had monstrous orthotics for which I had to buy different dancing shoes. Another $150.
Eventually I was able to find $19 nice little orthotics that fit any shoe.
My problem was a metatarsal pain, two bones that are to close, rubbing.
Eventually after 3-4 years of use all went back into place and I do not need anything.
(But on my weekly chiropractic adjustments, he also works on my feet.)

So, possibly chiropractor may be worth trying.

The $19 was just final price.
looking at my ($500) orthotics I was guessing and buying orthotics at amazon.
The third $19 worked.
Mine were totally made of plastic.
This is closest I can locate now that looks similar to what I have been using.

....
Pedag 2879 Holiday 3/4 Leather Ultra Light, Thin, Semi-Rigid Orthotic with Metatarsal Pad, Arch Support and Padding at the Heel, Black, Women's 8 EU 38

# Thread: Trees
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trees.10401/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-03-31T08:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trees.10401/post-118955>

Mentioned in a recent thread, weren't they?

I think it interesting that what I suppose would be the two most widely selling post-war children's authors in the English-speaking world (and beyond) seem to have tried to restore a sense of what you might call the numinousness of trees. (Safari doesn't like that word: changed it to "numerousness" LOL  )

> "Woe, woe, woe!" called the voice. "Woe for my brothers and sisters! Woe for the holy trees! The woods are laid waste. The axe is loosed against us. We are being felled. Great trees are falling, falling, falling."
>
> With the last "falling" the speaker came in sight. She was like a woman but so tall that her head was on a level with the Centaur's yet she was like a tree too. It is hard to explain if you have never seen a Dryad but quite unmistakable once you have - something different in the colour, the voice, and the hair. King Tirian and the two Beasts knew at once that she was the nymph of a beech tree ...
>
> Right through the middle of that ancient forest - that forest where the trees of gold and of silver had once grown and where a child from our world had once planted the Tree of Protection - a broad lane had already been opened. It was a hideous lane like a raw gash in the land, full of muddy ruts where felled trees had been dragged down to the river. There was a great crowd of people at work, and a cracking of whips, and horses tugging and straining as they dragged at the logs.

> They found that they were looking at a most extraordinary face. It belonged to a large Man-like, almost Troll-like, figure, at least fourteen foot high, very sturdy, with a tall head, and hardly any neck. Whether it was clad in stuff like green and grey bark, or whether that was its hide, was difficult to say. At any rate the arms, at a short distance from the trunk, were not wrinkled, but covered with a brown smooth skin. The large feet had seven toes each. The lower part of the long face was covered with a sweeping grey beard, bushy, almost twiggy at the roots, thin and mossy at the ends. But at the moment the hobbits noted little but the eyes. These deep eyes were now surveying them, slow and solemn, but very penetrating. They were brown, shot with a green light. Often afterwards Pippin tried to describe his first impression of them.
>
> 'One felt as if there was an enormous well behind them, filled up with ages of memory and long, slow, steady thinking; but their surface was sparkling with the present: like sun shimmering on the outer leaves of a vast tree, or on the ripples of a very deep lake. I don't know but it felt as if something that grew in the ground-asleep, you might say, or just feeling itself as something between roof-tip and leaf-tip, between deep earth and sky had suddenly waked up, and was considering you with the same slow care that it had given to its own inside affairs for endless years.'
>
> 'Hrum, Hoom,' murmured the voice, a deep voice like a very deep woodwind instrument. 'Very odd indeed! Do not be hasty, that is my motto.

I doubt I need to name the authors, who were, of course, friends. I think the second passage is more successful, at least in isolation, but then it comes from a book not primarily aimed at children, so can be more leisurely and complex.

I never got round to reading Tolkien's letters. There's apparently one in which he gets angry about a tree that was outside his window getting chopped down. It's like an old friend, not just a lump of wood.

Nice quotations from _The Nourishing Traditions Book of Baby and Child Care_:

> Traditional man would consider it absurd to merely objectively observe a tree, and to deny that the tree has intention and qualities
>
> This view changed slowly … until its culmination in the Renaissance period … when for the first time … humans began to see the stuff of the world as "just" a collection of molecules, essentially devoid of attributes. Modern man refers to the old outlook as "animism" … and best relegated to the dustbin of history ...

I think that's probably an exaggeration. It seems to me that some intellectuals thought like that, mostly rather later, and I think it really took till the 19th century before that view was dominant. But not everyone did even then: see how country people thought in, say, the novels of Thomas Hardy. There is, however, an important shift in how science as an activity is viewed in the writings of Francis Bacon (_Novum Organum Scientiarum_, 1620).

Slightly further on they continue

> The pre-eminent centrist and thinker of the early 1800s, Johnan Wolfgang von Goethe, did not embrace the mechanistic views that contemporaneous science adopted during his time, and he denied rationality's superiority as the sole interpreter of reality.

I shan't comment on Goethe: I don't read German. But I tend to think that Saruman's assumption of a many-coloured garment in the _Lord of the Rings_ is a reference to Goethe's writings on the rainbow and colour, which I'm sure Tolkien must have read and talked about. Saruman, who has discarded his white cloak, can't see that sometimes a whole is greater than the sum of its parts. There's surely a link between this and his destructiveness.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: LiFi
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifi.10404/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-03-31T12:35:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifi.10404/post-119029>

I hadn't heard of this before ...

> CEA-Leti will demonstrate its new prototype for wireless high data rate Li-Fi (light fidelity) transmission at Light + Building 2014 in Frankfurt, Germany, March 30-April 4. The technology employs the high-frequency modulation capabilities of light-emitting diode (LED) engines used in commercial lighting. It achieves throughputs of up to 10Mb/s at a range of three meters, suitable for HD video streaming or Internet browsing, using light power of less than 1,000 lumens and with direct or even indirect lighting.

This is the bit that caught my eye:

> Other technical and market factors also are increasing interest in data transmission through lighting. These include crowding of the conventional radiofrequency (RF) spectrum, the mobile data-traffic explosion in cellular networks, **and the need for wireless data transmission without electromagnetic field (EMF) interference.**

There's a tacit admission that EMF is problematic ... even if only in the context of too much in the spectrum already.

[http://www.nanotech-now.com/news.cgi?story\_id=49241](http://www.nanotech-now.com/news.cgi?story_id=49241)

### sooperb — 2014-03-31T12:41:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifi.10404/post-119031>

Thanks for that snippet, I will be floating that past doubting Thomas!

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-31T12:57:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifi.10404/post-119032>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=119031)
>
> Thanks for that snippet, I will be floating that past doubting Thomas!

_Caveat --_ I take it by "interference" they mean with other EMF signals and devices not with biological systems. Of course, we know both are possible ...

It seems to me all you really have to do to raise the possibility is to show that at the cellular level communication is electronic. After that, once you start to look at the evidence ...

The piece that someone recently tweeted to Jack is chilling -- and that is based on a study published in the British Medical Journal not just ... well you know what people say.

[https://twitter.com/fredgabberline/status/449337496422600705](https://twitter.com/fredgabberline/status/449337496422600705)

Short piece in the Independent on LiFi. It's British technology.  I didn't know anything was these days!

[http://www.independent.co.uk/news/s...-250-times-faster-than-broadband-8909320.html](http://www.independent.co.uk/news/science/lifi-revolution-internet-connections-using-light-bulbs-are-250-times-faster-than-broadband-8909320.html)

Note:

> the technology would also be of use in situations where radio frequencies cannot be used for fear of interfering with electronic circuitry.

_That's_ what they're worried about. If RF transmissions bugger up someone's brain so what?

It's not going to replace existing technology:

> One draw-back is that the data receiver would have to be in sight of the transmitter-bulb as visible light does not penetrate solid materials.

Kind of interesting, anyway. More here:

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li-Fi](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li-Fi)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-schools-act-against-mobile-phones.10405/post-119036>

It's not the EMF; its the distraction they represent:

> Schools in the Truro area are taking measures to control the increasing numbers of children using mobile phones.
>
> Latest survey results show nearly nine in ten (88 per cent) of 12-14 year olds now own a mobile phone.
> Schools insist on pupils keeping their phones out of sight but face a difficult battle with children intent on using the mobile devices.
>
> The survey by Halifax Insurance said the study found today’s children each own an average of £924 worth of electronic devices, and a third (33 per cent) check their phone for messages several times an hour ...
>
> \[At some schools in Truro\] no mobile phones are allowed but if they are brought to school they must be switched off.
>
> A spokesman for the school said: “If a pupil breaches the school policy then the mobile phone will be confiscated and handed to the head teacher." ...

[http://www.westbriton.co.uk/Schools...le-phone-use/story-20878542-detail/story.html](http://www.westbriton.co.uk/Schools-Truro-area-act-control-mobile-phone-use/story-20878542-detail/story.html)

# Thread: High histamine / heartburn

### BJK77 — 2014-03-31T16:31:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-histamine-heartburn.10407/post-119055>

I'm in a tailspin lately (stress of quitting my job is probably making things worse, I'm sure). I've suddenly developed major histamine problems where everything I eat (since it's all high histamine - organ meats, seafood, etc) is causing me to burn up like crazy or shiver like mad!

It seems I start out shivering in my chest in the mornings after my BAB and sometimes my neck and face will be burning up at the same time. As the day goes on, I just ROAST. My face and ears turn bright red and are literally hot to the touch. I read on Amy Yasko's site that high histamine is associated with high levels of stomach acid, which I suppose could explain my sudden heartburn.

I know this is all related to poor redox. I'm working on that, but does anyone have any great tips for trying to manage these issues in the meantime? I'm literally getting about 2 hours of sleep at night because I am just HOT and can't get comfortable. I keep the window open and last night it dropped to 27 degrees and I was still burning up. I'm putting ice packs on my carotid arteries at night. I take 500 mg Quercetin with each meal, but none of this is helping and the heartburn is getting worse and more painful.

I actually had heartburn this morning at 5 AM and I hadn't even eaten since 5 PM the night before.

Good thoughts Inger! Earthing outside is the only thing that stops the heart racing. I spent most of yesterday afternoon out there to try to calm things down and it did help. This morning it's raining and I spent a good hour sitting under a tree reading. It was wonderful, but I can't do that all day every day. I need to figure out what's causing this.

Fresh is hard. I try to thaw my meats and cook them right away saving in the fridge only what I'll eat for the next two days and freezing the rest. It is really tough with meats and fish though.

I'm really stuck with veggies too because I seem to be so limited - no high oxalate veggies, no sulfur veggies. That doesn't leave me with much. That's why I had been relying on lots of avocado, but now I see that is high histamine! I did try a little butternut squash this morning with my breakfast, but I'm still getting the heart racing. Unfortunately we only just got rid of all the snow covering the ground, so I don't have anything wild coming up yet

I will post what I'm eating/supplementing and see if anyone has ideas as to what's going on. Like I said, this seems to be the WORST after my BAB.

**BAB**
\- usually 50 gm+ protein from pork roast
\- pork fat, lard

**Lunch**
\- 6 raw oysters
\- 1/2 - 1 avocado

**Dinner**
\- scallops

These have been my "go-to" meals for the last couple of weeks. I honestly don't know what else to eat with my limitations! Everytime I try to go higher fat I crash pretty hard. My legs get weak and shakey and I just don't feel well.

**Supplements**
Naturethroid: 3/4 mcg 2x/day

**With BAB:**
\- Vitamin D - 12,000 IU
\- Vitamin K - 15 mg
\- D-Ribose - 15 mg
\- CoQ10 - 300 mg
\- Quercetin - 500 mg
\- Yucca - 1000 mg
\- Welchol - 2 tablets
\- Molybdenum - 150 mcg

**Lunch**
\- 500 mg Quercetin
\- 2 Welchol tablets

**Dinner**
\- 500 mg Quercetin
\- 2 Welchol tablets

Any thoughts on whether any of these supplements could be a problem?

# Thread: Nassim Haramein, the Einstein of our generation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nassim-haramein-the-einstein-of-our-generation.10411/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BradTheBreather — 2014-03-31T20:52:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nassim-haramein-the-einstein-of-our-generation.10411/post-119104>

I recent stumbled across some incredible ideas about the fundamental nature of the universe. Nassim Haramein offers a framework for a unified theory that solves Einstein's field equations at the scale of a proton, eliminating the need for things like dark matter, dark energy, and the strong and weak nuclear force.

I have only a basic understanding of physics but as I have been exploring, it has really been blowing my mind so I felt the need to share it with some people that might actually listen...

The tip of the iceberg of implications: free energy, extraterrestrial involvement in human evolution, telepathy, teleportation....

The video that introduced me to it:

The website of the Nassim's research center in Hawaii:
[http://resonance.is/](http://resonance.is/)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-31T21:44:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nassim-haramein-the-einstein-of-our-generation.10411/post-119111>

[http://thrivedebunked.wordpress.com/2012/03/09/who-is-nassim-haramein/](http://thrivedebunked.wordpress.com/2012/03/09/who-is-nassim-haramein/)

I have gotten at least 20 emails from people about him.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-31T21:44:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nassim-haramein-the-einstein-of-our-generation.10411/post-119112>

metaphysics does not = QED

### BradTheBreather — 2014-04-02T17:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nassim-haramein-the-einstein-of-our-generation.10411/post-119413>

Its easy to find reactions to extraordinary ideas. Does this eliminate the need to maintain an open mind and really explore them? I know this critique of you is mostly name calling and ridiculous but I post it to raise a point. Anyone (not just Nassim) who will present the radical shift in viewpoint that is required to create and understand a potential unification theory is going to have plenty of dissenters, and the presence of opposing arguments shouldn't make us ignore what could be a new way of looking at things.

You say it yourself all the time, "the truth will set you free, but the first time you hear it, it will piss you off".

This Bob-a-thon guy attacks Haramein, saying he doesn't have any physics credentials, while not presenting any reason to believe that Bob-a-tho knows anything about physics. I believe a lot can be done in the world of theoretical physics and mathematics outside the mainstream institutions, but I actually read Bob's critique and I get the sense that he is just a sexually-frustrated physics grad student in his late 20s. Even with my modest 6-8 hours of investigation into Haramein's work, I can tell that Bob-a-thon is misunderstanding some of the basic concepts and still very attached to his comfortable way of thinking in the standard model. When he tries to debunk the actual physics of Haramein, I'm not convinced. Most of the text is name calling and when he actually does bring in some reasoning, its something like "this doesn't agree with anything before" or "this is way off from the established laws"...

If it were aligned with what has been done, we would have already solved unification. Its easy to get caught up in the idea that we already know everything (or that we can _know_ anything for that matter), but really at the end of the day, even the things that appear most solid in the world are really made up of mostly empty space.

Haramein responded to Bob-a-thons arguments and the usual critiques of his work at great length here: [http://web.archive.org/web/20111110...nceproject.org/schwarzschild-proton-manifesto](http://web.archive.org/web/20111110193243/http://theresonanceproject.org/schwarzschild-proton-manifesto)
Please devote at least the same amount of time to this article as you did to Bob-a-thons and then I will hear what you have to say.

Jack, I have an incredible amount of respect for you and your ability to do what you know is right, even when it goes against everything that has gone before, and a lot of your recommendations have very greatly helped me on my personal health journey and my understanding of my own health. That said, things are different now than when you first started. You are not fighting as much to have people listen to you. Instead you have over 9000 people just waiting to read every word you put on this forum, and any dissenters are immediately quieted by your disciples. If I were in your position, it would be easy to use my own incredibly vast and complex way of thinking to justify anything I want as well as disprove anything I want. Part of what makes you great is how much you build and interact with your community and answer every question you can, but part of what _made_ you great in the first place was questioning what you yourself had thought all your life...

Personally, science is not my only means of finding truth. I respect it for sure, but I acknowledge its limitations in affecting my subjective experience (which is all that really matters when you come down to it). Spend a couple hours in a flotation tank or take a trip down to Peru to participate in an Ayahuasca ritual and you will see there is much more out there than can be measured with a lab test. When I first learned about the more philosophical side of QED, it really resonated with me and explained a lot of things in my life. That's why I love your work with applying it to health. It simply makes sense on many levels.

When I first heard Haramein's arguments, they just made everything click. A unification theory won't be a complex physics equation, it will be a simple idea that not only unifies QED with relativistic physics, but unifies all branches of science and unifies science with philosophy, spirituality, art, music, history, archaeology and everything else there is. Haramein's model is in the early stages of development obviously, but for me that was enough to see unification of a lot of things in my own life.

I will conclude with this quote from chapter 12 of the Tao Te Ching (Stephen Mitchell translation)

"Colors blind the eye.
Sounds deafen the ear.
Flavors numb the taste.
Thoughts weaken the mind.
Desires wither the heart.

The Master observes the world
but trusts his inner vision.
He allows things to come and go.
His heart is as open as the sky."

### BradTheBreather — 2014-04-02T18:25:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nassim-haramein-the-einstein-of-our-generation.10411/post-119423>

Besides, you're the one who talks about crystals and vortex energies... Maybe there is a QED basis for some metaphysical concepts...

### Josh — 2014-04-02T18:44:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nassim-haramein-the-einstein-of-our-generation.10411/post-119427>

From the bridge, we are children in playing in a sandbox....consider....

> And the way it has been written is such that those who are not ready for it will turn their back
> automatically. A similar case has happened in this century with Gurdjieff. For thirty years
> continuously, he was preparing one book. A man of the caliber of Gurdjieff can do that work in
> three days. Even three days may be more than enough. Lao Tzu had done it: in three days the
> whole TAO TEH CHING was written. Gurdjieff can do it in three days; there is no difficulty. But for
> thirty years he was writing his first book. And what he was doing? He will write one chapter, and then
> he will allow it to be read before his disciples. Disciples will be listening to the chapter, and he will
> be looking to the disciples. If they can understand, he will change it. That was the condition: if they
> can understand, he will change it. If he sees that they are following it, then it is wrong. Continuously,
> for thirty years, every chapter was read a thousand and one times, and every time he was watching.
> When the book became completely impossible, that no one can read and understand it...
> Even a very intelligent person will have to read it at least seven times; then glimpses of meaning will
> start coming. And that too will be just glimpses. If he wants to penetrate more, then he will have to
> practice whatsoever he has said, and through practice meanings will become clear. And it will take
> at least one’s whole life to come to the total understanding what he has written.
> This type of book cannot be interpolated. Really, his first book, it is said that very few people have
> read it completely. It is difficult – one thousand pages. So when the first edition was published, he
> published it with a condition: only hundred pages, the introductory part, were cut. All other pages
> were not cut; they were uncut. Only hundred pages were cut, and a note was given on the book
> that ”If you can read the first hundred pages and still think to read ahead, then cut other pages.
> Otherwise, return the book to the publisher and take your money back.”
> It is said that there are very few people alive who have read the whole book completely. It is written
> in such a way that you will get fed up. Reading twenty, twenty-five pages, it is enough; and this man
> seems to be mad.

Osho, Yoga The Alpha and the Omega Vol 1, #6

It took me 5 times to penetrate into Gurdjieff and then it broke my heart open, I do not buy easy as a path to anything beyond a glimpse....

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-02T19:18:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nassim-haramein-the-einstein-of-our-generation.10411/post-119432>

I question everything..........because my curiosity requires it. But I also know when the boundaries have been stretched with out foundation.........and I call BS on things I dont buy. You can not know good without knowing bad.........balance in knowledge and wisdom are the key.

### Shijin13 — 2014-04-01T17:23:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-review-help.10412/post-119243>

HDL is low, and Trigs are High you need to get them flipped around. you want HDL above 60 and Trigs below 60. Your LDL is high b/c of pregnenolone steal & b/c your vitamin A cycle is off - which is causing the low pregnenolone levels. Your thyroid isn't working well even with the Levothyroxine. FWIW some of the generics of Levothyroxine contains gluten. I'd bet you're making Reverse T3 instead of Free T3 - which also impacts your conversion of LDL to Pregnenolone... which is why your testosterone is in the tank (and is also why your DHEA will be in the tank)

Next time ask your doc for a TSH, Free T4, Free T3, and Reverse T3. Make sure you also get a Vit D3 - 25 hydroxy run too...

Start working on the 3 legged stool, and cut all carbs out to begin working on the HDL and Trigs. you probably also need to consider supplementation with Pregnenolone. As JanSz - I'm not a doctor! However I am a biohacker and I can read labs...

### Shijin13 — 2014-04-01T19:16:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-review-help.10412/post-119268>

JNV read Hormones 101 and Hormones 102
[http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/](http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/)
[http://jackkruse.com/quantum-bio-10-hormone-102-drawn-to-light/](http://jackkruse.com/quantum-bio-10-hormone-102-drawn-to-light/)

You've got the TSH but T3 update and T4 total - don't tell the story. Good docs will run a Free T3 and Free T4, as well as Reverse T3. - this tells you how your Thyroid is making T4, and converting it to either Free T3 or Reverse T3.

As for carbs - stick to leafy greens, summer squashes. Peas are legumes so you should drop them too. Corn and potatoes should be kicked to the curb - and are why your trigs are so high. If you're going to have starchy carbs - limit them to fall/winter squashes/roots during fall - and in limited qtys.

Limit your fruit as well. if you want fruit consider wild edibles... such as wild raspberries, blackberries, blueberries, mulberries, gooseberries and crabapples...

\=======================================================
1grain of Natural Thyroid from pigs=(T4-38mcg)+(T3-9mcg)=38+36=74mcg-T4 equivalent
1mcg-T3=4mcg-T4
9mcg-T3=36mcg-T4 equivalent

Consider recalculating your current dose of Levothyroxine=T4 converting it to equivalent NTH, and ask your doc for permission to use it
\===========================================================================
CORTISOL AM: 11.3 / 3.7 - 19.4 ug/dl
PREGNENOLONE: 20 /<121 ng/dL

Both are low.
Adjust your daily life so it is in agreement with Circadian Rhythm.
If you live above 40degree lattide supplement with 10000iu/day vit D
also A & K2

Get 4xsaliva test to figure out your current cortisol profile.

Likely you are also low in progesterone
Consider pregnenolone supplementation sufficient to raise it to
preg~180ng/dL
prog (1.4-4)ng/dL

After you get there re-evaluate thyroid panel
\============================================================================
You tested DHEA
get also DHEAs
most everybody is low on DHEAs, start with 50mg/day supplementation
aim at DHEAs(350-490) μg/dL
\============================================================================
You are low in Total Testosterone
you may want to check LH & FSH, specially if you want preserve fertility.
consider supplementation with testosterone and HCG.
there are two major types of testosterone supplementation,
transdermal and injectable
your goal is to keep TT, DHT, E2 at good levels
with SHBG~17 you want to raise TT to about 700-900ng/dL
to improve erections you want DHT(75-100)ng/dL
erection also heavily depend on E2 & progesterone levels
transdermal testosterone works better when it is thin (1%), the higher density may work but the higher the % the more problematic it becomes
thyroid problems affect skin permeability to testosterone
transdermal testosterone goes thru 5aR within skin, that converts testosterone to DHT, dihydro-testosterone
the thinner testosterone the more area must be used the higher DHT raises
it is all balancing act
injectable testosterone is surer way to raise testosterone, have its own set of problems
easiest to use and figure out proper dose is testosterone cypionate or enantathe
but injections should be done at least weekly, preferably EOD, E3D or 2x/week
Good starting dose for your SHBG level would be 40mg/EOD=140mg/week on average
sometimes combination of Cypionate & Androgel is need (Androgel-1% to raise DHT)
Androgel-1% alone tends to raise DHT sky high
newer ever ever ever use 5Ar inhibitors (finasteride, duosteride, Avodart, Proscar, Propecia, herbals containing such actions).

\===========================================================================

250# weight
get scale that measures body fat (Ozeri sold on Amazon)
aim at 10-15% body fat
you must loose lots of weight
at this time strenuous exercises may hurt you, but long hours of CT are your friend
being allergic to gluten helps you, skip also any carbohydrates that raise insulin and blood glucose
review Jacks Resistant Starch blog, make best use of it.
Practically most nuts are suspect that they are heat treated and therefore worthless as a source of resistant starch, it may be hard to follow that blog.
Keep in back pocket info on unmodified potato starch (4 tablespoons/day). Some claim good results.
There is a strong correlation between resistant starches and your high insulin and glucose.

\=============================================================================
CRP HIGH SENSITIVITY: 3.40 / <7.48 mg/L

fatty acids are highly influential in controlling inflammation
most popular are discussions of omega6/omega3 ratios.
Those discussions often are lacking enough information that would allow precise follow up testing
Also omega7 are responsible for inflammation management

Without getting into more details, safe way is to avoid all fish oil and krill oil supplementation
instead
divide in half your weekly allotment meats and make sure that for each pound of pork of beef you eat a found of oysters, shrimps and fish

I am not a doctor.

Your #1 problem is to clarify your pituitary status.
Pituitary rules many processes that we talk about.
Eventually you should monitor all of your pituitary hormones for a period of time sufficient to figure out what kind help/intervention they need to steer your body in optimal way.
Until you are not clear on your pituitary,
any cortisol, thyroid, testosterone interventions are temporary and subject to drastic change.
Likely it may be wise to not take any supplements (over the counter) or medicines (prescription) unless specifically advised by the doctor who is helping you with your pituitary. Taking them on your own may obscure evaluation of your pituitary.

Since you have a doctor who figured out your pituitary problems,
consider sticking with him for your other heath problems.
Pituitary is one of the master switches, doctor who can figure out your pituitary have mostly proved himself to you.
So for now it would be a good idea to give that doctor enough time to fix you up best he can.
\=================================================

(Generally) You have number of ways going about finding another doctor.
Friendly general practitioner, most often MD but osteopath may be better. If he have open mind and allows you to go your way supporting it with scripts for medicine and tests, that may be a keeper.

Doctor who know more about natural healing, review Shijin13's webinar on how to find doctor. One of the ways would be to check with Genowa Diagnostics Laboratory asking them for names of doctors who order their labs.
Checking with local compounding pharmacy asking for docs ordering their products.
Best would be if you could hire one of the doctors that shows up on dr Kruses presentations.

Practically no doctor can help you without your cooperation.
So, it will be easier for you to take good advice if you have at least general understanding of where your problem may be. That is likely main reason why dr Kruse is educating all of us.
\=====================
Good Circadian Rhythm is one of the basics, you live or die because of it.
You will newer be able to correct anything, specially the extra 90# of fat that you are carrying around without improvements in Circadian Rhythm.

To support or reestablish good Circadian rhythm:

Live like people lived before invention of electricity, preferably 500-5000 years before that.

wakeup at dawn, right before sunrise (you have been previous 8 hrs in totally dark room)
barefoot and naked walk on grass or sandy beach (look up Caroline and our other girls, they love watching)
look at the sun 2-3 minutes (note when sun just rises it is not as bright as latter on)
look at bright part of the sky if cloudy (shuts off melatonin, reinforces cortisol)
do that snow, wind, rain or shine,
preferably
take shower in cold water
within 30-60 min from wakeup eat 50% of your daily food

lunch

dinner ends 4 hrs before bed time

sundown, no electric lights, specially in blue spectrum, red and amber are ok (camp fire), use flux.com to change brightness of computer screen
sleep within 10PM-8AM window

\====================

> proteins=(min 1/2 pound/day) of shellfish from mollusks and crustaceans, balance beef, pork, (birds eggs only),
> worst farm raised seafood is better than best grass fed beef

> Following dr Kruse, I would summarize diet as:
> No grains whatsoever, no potatoes & sugar. (But look up caveat, hot potato, cold potato, resistant starch)
> Carbohydrates -- only minimal amount and not in winter
> Protein=30% of daily caloric intake
> Fat=70% of daily caloric intake<<<<<<<<<<=============================
> Protein part=70%seafood(DHA) + 30%(pork, beef, no birds except eggs)(AA)
>
> To loose weight, from above cut out some fat, if hungry replace that with additional seafood.
> And maximize CT

Problems with fish oils.

1----it is commercial product that went thru number of processes before it got into bottle or gelcap.
contains extremely high amount of EPA in relation to DHA. Using fish/krill oils you will always have excess EPA.

2---- Some claim that the DHA in fish/kril oils is not the real DHA that we want, but it is missing some electrons (otherwise about the same), but we do care a lot about electrons their spin and inclination and coherence.
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-2](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-2)

3----large amounts of EPA + DHA (or any other omega3) will suppress omega6, specially AA.
omega3-resist inflammation
omega6-promotes inflammation
HS-CRP ->> = one of the inflammations indicators
we want omega6/omega3 in balance (to create inflammation/anti-inflammation balance)
that balance is evaluated many different ways
most known (and least clear) is O6/O3 ratio
least prone to counterfeit and obscurity is:

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
done at MayoClinic or LabCorp

best known to me is:

[http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/](http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/)
BodyBio Wellness Report
BodyBio Professional Report (Doctor's Report)
BodyBio Fatty Acid Report (Doctor's Report) <<<<<===================

\=============

Corrections are done mostly by manipulating diet.

\==========================================
There is a zillion of articles about fish and krill oils.
There is a possibility that all claims are correct for very narrow and specific applications.
All that is forgotten, and fish and krill oils flow is likely bigger than flow of statins.

Ed and Patricia Kane (fatty acids experts) are the lonely voice against indiscriminate fish/krill oil use.
But I go with what they say.

For what is worth, dr Kruse refered me to Patricia Kane as a good source of information on fatty acids. But dr Kruse still sells fish and krill oils, so take all this, digest it, and use what makes sense to you. Nothing is really cast in stone.

**BUT PER LEF.ORG CLAIMS, your high insulin and highish glucose could use some omega7 (and resistant starch),
but settle first your GH/pituitary issue.**

bodybio.com
[http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory129.aspx](http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory129.aspx)

> Next time ask your doc for a TSH, Free T4, Free T3, and Reverse T3. Make sure you also get a Vit D3 - 25 hydroxy run too...
>
> Start working on the 3 legged stool, and cut all carbs out to begin working on the HDL and Trigs. you probably also need to consider supplementation with Pregnenolone. As JanSz - I'm not a doctor! However I am a biohacker and I can read labs...

Shinjin13 - with my updated labs, were you correct in saying: "I'd bet you're making Reverse T3 instead of Free T3..."

There is two way to tend to a gut flora, may be three.
1\. support and feed existing gut flora so it multiplies ----- eat proper food, resistant starch helps here
2\. eat food containing gut flora ---- sauerkraut, kimchi, raw food
3\. supplement with pills containing gut flora, butyrate pills, inulin, pre-biotics...

I am not sure what safe starch is.
Resistant starch is a starch that resist digestion by human body but is a food for beneficial bugs residing in our body.
Those bugs grow in number in presence of resistant starch.

Recently Ameer Rosic had webinar on this topic. Mostly I remember potatoes, rice, beans, plantans, green bananas and unmodified potato starch.
Unmodified potato starch is ready to eat from the bag as long as is not heated.
Potato and rice are cooked as usual, when hot they contain starch that we here do not care about,
but,
when you cool them to below 50F previously digestible starch converts to resistant starch.
German potato salad is good representative of how to get this type of starch in the meal.

I understand that all this may not be exactly what dr Kruse is preaching but he supports Ameer so it may be acceptable alternative.

Sometimes it is hard for me to figure what dr Kruse supports and what not.
Often it is on case by case basis, same key can be the latest cat's meau and again same key can open the door too hell.
So whatever action you decide to take, it is all you. Just make sure to pick the correct bread crumbs and you are going to be ok.

Likely the best would be to do testing to monitor progress and figure out where you need some help at given time.
glucose
insulin
stool analysis

[http://www.allthingsmale.com/forum/showthread.php?23961-Resistant-starch](http://www.allthingsmale.com/forum/showthread.php?23961-Resistant-starch)
[http://perfecthealthdiet.com/the-diet/](http://perfecthealthdiet.com/the-diet/)

[http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory97.aspx](http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory97.aspx)

[http://www.bodybio.com/storeproduct361.aspx](http://www.bodybio.com/storeproduct361.aspx)

# Thread: Aluminum Cans and Seafood
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aluminum-cans-and-seafood.10415/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8

### kyrakitty — 2014-04-01T01:19:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aluminum-cans-and-seafood.10415/post-119127>

I do eat fresh raw oysters as often as I can but it isn't always possible. Smoked oysters in a can are usually packed in aluminum with olive oil not spring water. I have been having a can 5-6 days a week. I really like eating smoked oysters in the can they taste soooo yummy to me. I know a lot of people can't stand them BUT...

QUESTION: Is this doing more harm than good due to the aluminum cans and/or the oysters not being fresh?

### Tanya — 2014-04-01T03:01:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aluminum-cans-and-seafood.10415/post-119150>

> [kyrakitty said:](/goto/post?id=119127)
>
> I do eat fresh raw oysters as often as I can but it isn't always possible. Smoked oysters in a can are usually packed in aluminum with olive oil not spring water. I have been having a can 5-6 days a week. I really like eating smoked oysters in the can they taste soooo yummy to me. I know a lot of people can't stand them BUT...
>
> QUESTION: Is this doing more harm than good due to the aluminum cans and/or the oysters not being fresh?

I don't know what brand you buy, but if it is Crown Prince oysters, for example, they are not on their list of products in BPA free cans. Another thing to consider [http://www.crownprince.com/bpa-free-cans.htm](http://www.crownprince.com/bpa-free-cans.htm)

### kyrakitty — 2014-04-01T03:30:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aluminum-cans-and-seafood.10415/post-119152>

Aie ya yai! Yes that stinking BPA. You are so right great reminder thank you for looking out Tanya. That is yet another thing on all our lists to have to find products without them.

Well the good news, if there is such a thing with BPA, is that we are clear BPA is bad for you so it is easy to do research to find canned items without it.

However, what I am extremely unclear on AND I have scoured the world wide web AND this forum is the aluminum issue. If I eat a lot of canned fish products like smoked oysters, mackerel, sardiens and if it is BPA free but is all packed in aluminum Is this doing more harm than good due to the aluminum cans? I don't want to have aluminum getting stored up in my brain or anywhere else in my body. Coca cola can eat through anything so I can see why aluminum and soda is a bad bad deal but does the same hold true for fish packed in olive oil or spring water?

### Tanya — 2014-04-01T03:48:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aluminum-cans-and-seafood.10415/post-119154>

If I eat a lot of canned fish products like smoked oysters, mackerel, sardiens and if it is BPA free but is all packed in aluminum Is this doing more harm than good due to the aluminum cans? I don't want to have aluminum getting stored up in my brain or anywhere else in my body. Coca cola can eat through anything so I can see why aluminum and soda is a bad bad deal but does the same hold true for fish packed in olive oil or spring water?

I see what you mean..... I could not find anything specifically about the aluminum either. However, canned fish is an oily product (greater risk there) and heated in the can for sterilization purposes (another risk)... I might need to stop using canned fish as much as I do now

### Tanya — 2014-04-01T16:03:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aluminum-cans-and-seafood.10415/post-119214>

Still thinking about it, if the aluminum cans have the BPA free lining, it should hopefully reduce the risk of aluminum getting transferred into the food.....

### kyrakitty — 2014-04-01T18:49:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aluminum-cans-and-seafood.10415/post-119258>

### Tanya — 2014-04-01T18:56:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aluminum-cans-and-seafood.10415/post-119263>

### kyrakitty — 2014-04-01T19:11:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aluminum-cans-and-seafood.10415/post-119267>

What's even more interesting, that I've noticed, is that producers go to great lengths to seemingly hide the materials used in the can.

If you go to their websites most will not list the materials of the cans. They list BPA free or not and if it is cooked in the can or not but in my paranoia most seemingly stay far away from listing their cans as tin or aluminum. You have to call to find out if it's tin or aluminum. I am sure I just made up this conspiracy theory but it sure feels true when I am researching and hitting dead ends. LOL

# Thread: True Thirst?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2014-04-01T11:40:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/post-119194>

Hey,

So in today's world, many people who are cellularly dehydrated don't feel dehydrate, ie don't feel thirsty. This is probably because the environment we live in is messing with our bodies signals and our bodies general health is usually bad, with messed up hormones, etc. This all means it cannot sense the dehydration.
Or maybe once your body is kicked out of using water for energy and is forced to use ATP, it craves ATP instead, as this is what it can use for energy right now.

Either way our true thirst signal to refill out nano-tubes is absent. I can think of 2 ways to get this back. The first is grounding and sunlight. Jack said this and there has been some anecdotal evidence of these forums.

The second is fasting. It is very common when water fasting to experience a dry mouth. On the things I have read about water fasting it says that the dry mouth goes away after a few weeks (or however long it takes) and then true hunger returns (you lose your hunger on the fast). I did not personally experience this but I was doing it wrong. All the other fasting symptoms (detox) go away at this point.

So I think what is happening there is the fast awakens your true thirst by forcing you into ketosis and forcing you to use water as an energy source again. After a few weeks when you have drunk enough water to refill your nano tubes, the dry mouth stops, detox is finished and hunger returns.

I have recently dropped my morning meal and am now just eating an evening one. This has cut my calories roughly in half. I have upped my water intake by ~1.5L. Upon doing this I noticed my body started doing the same reactions it did when I last did water fasting (weakness, dizziness, dry mouth, sore throat, flem, bad breath, etc). So my body is treating this 24hr between food thing like it does a proper fast. Anyway I have decreased my food intake (incl. protein) and increased my water so I should be feeling more hydrated however I have been having more dry mouth and the top of my throat is soar and rough every night. This indicates true thirst is coming back somewhat as I'm feeling more dehydrated. Or it could be that I am more dehydrated because my body is using more of that water I'm drinking as energy.

Anyway my question is, can you force your body into the PPP / using water as it's main source of energy if you give it no other option? Ie, take away the food so it cannot use ATP and replace it with extra water.

Part 2, does hypothyroid have some benefits with regards to entering the PPP? As in having a lower core body temperature which would improve water chemistry.

### seanb4 — 2014-04-14T12:18:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/post-121411>

Update: Lasted about a week with the lower calories but couldn't maintain it. Started to eat more at my main meal and fasting symptoms went away. I think this could be because my body was now relying more on ATP instead of water for energy, it could also be because the extra food was causing more inflammation in my body via the stress response I get after eating.

### Lahelada — 2014-04-14T12:38:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/post-121413>

### Jonathin — 2014-04-14T18:05:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/post-121439>

ATP is necessary to unfold proteins so that they can be hydrolyzed by water. I am not sure if ATP is necessary once they are in that state however it seems that once any amount of dehydration occurs ATP is once again needed. It seems like a chicken and egg thing - there is no beginning and end but all factors need to be in place for things to work.

# Thread: Technology Killer
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/technology-killer.10420/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2014-04-01T16:17:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/technology-killer.10420/post-119218>

[http://www.airtrafficmanagement.net/2014/04/lithium-cargo-facing-outright-ban/](http://www.airtrafficmanagement.net/2014/04/lithium-cargo-facing-outright-ban/)

LI batteries power everything tech from your laptop, to cellphones, tablets, toys etc. back in 2010 - there was a crash of a UPS plane that was carrying a full load of these batteries. If you look at apple products 2010 was the last year the had a BIG product release.

Apple knew the score and has spent the past 4 years working on a new manufacturing and supply chain process, changing its entire mfr and supply chain models. Right now I'd be looking at 3 things - what type of battery have they developed that gives the same power as LI, whats the first product its going to be in, and how much $$$ is apple going to make off making batteries.

Its going to be interesting to see how the tech industry is going to get around this. I don't think they can. especially if they every find MH370 and the 500KG of batteries the a/c was carrying was the source of the accident.

computers, smartphones, and ipads are going to be come status symbols people won't be able to afford them any more.

this might be a way out.

### nonchalant — 2014-04-02T15:04:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/technology-killer.10420/post-119392>

They'll just start making potassium batteries... but those would probably be dangerous as well.

My uncle is a keen golfer. He plays at Moor Park. Unfortunately, he now has several medical problems, including diabetes, and found his form went after he was recovering from a couple of big operations. This, apparently, meant he was no longer a "fox" but a "rabbit" (hope I have the names right). By Christmas his handicap had improved again. He ended up going to the foxes' dinner and the rabbits' dinner. His wife said, "Surely, you can't go to both dinners; you must be one or the other?" He said, "I've been invited to both, and I'm attending any dinner I can get!"

# Thread: Medieval Danish latrines unearthed
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/medieval-danish-latrines-unearthed.10423/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-04-01T21:06:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/medieval-danish-latrines-unearthed.10423/post-119284>

> A number of Medieval wooden barrels have been uncovered in Denmark, revealing their less- than-glamorous contents.
>
> Originally built to transport goods and store fish, the barrels were converted into latrines — still filled with their original contents. …
>
> Described as being in "excellent condition," the human excrement can give scientists unique insight into what people ate in Denmark in the Middle Ages.
>
> "Preliminary results of analysis show that raspberries were popular in Odense in the 1300s." … \[Maria Elisabeth\] Lauridsen, \[the archaeologist in charge of the excavation\] said. ...

[http://news.discovery.com/history/archaeology/medieval-poop-still-smelling-bad-found-140401.htm](http://news.discovery.com/history/archaeology/medieval-poop-still-smelling-bad-found-140401.htm)

Also:

[http://cphpost.dk/news/archaeologists-make-smelly-discovery-in-odense.8916.html](http://cphpost.dk/news/archaeologists-make-smelly-discovery-in-odense.8916.html)

### Shijin13 — 2014-04-02T01:33:22+0300

# Thread: Do you think this RO is as good as it gets?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-this-ro-is-as-good-as-it-gets.10424/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kyrakitty — 2014-04-01T21:32:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-this-ro-is-as-good-as-it-gets.10424/post-119291>

So Crystal Springs home water delivery was a dud for me here in California because they add salts to improve flavor and every other home bottler does the same but then I stumbled upon Culligan. This is a national company and the local sales reps have local bottlers that produce RO water without added salts/minerals.

Attached is the water analysis report. Everything on it they tested for says ND no detectable traces except the minute amounts listed below. I know either way we need to periodically test the water ourselves to make sure they are living up to the advertised standards but based on the attached report and the few detectable traces I think this is the best bet for RO water. Notice the PH is 4.8 not 7 very interesting….

So, if you are in the US and having problems finding RO water without added minerals I bet Culligan can help but just to be sure I suggest you ask each supplier “do you offer RO water without adding minerals” if they say yes then ask your local Culligan rep to provide a water analysis. 877-386-0823

FYI the website lists almost zero details about their home water delivery service instead the site rambles on about water softners and installing water filtration systems. So if you want info on home delivery water service call cuz you won’t find anything online. 877-386-0823

I think this is as good as it gets for bottled home delivery and if I test it and it matches then I hit a home run. Any thoughts and comments would be appreciated if anything is missing that I should ask for from this bottler.

A few notable details:
\-Municipal water turned into RO water that does not have added minerals by the bottler
\-Sample date 3/25/2013
\-The following was detectable on the attached PDF:

PAGE 2
Chloride = .6
Copper = ND but then one line below it continues with copper stats & says.
Est TDS by Cond = 1.0

PAGE 9
Conductivity = 2
PH = 4.8 (not exactly neutral or 7)

PAGE 11
Sodium (Na) 0.1

PAGE 12
Potassium (K) 0.2

### kyrakitty — 2014-04-01T21:32:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-this-ro-is-as-good-as-it-gets.10424/post-119292>

### Melanie Procter — 2014-04-02T04:01:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ppp.10425/post-119351>

I am very interested in the topic of glutathione production. I think I am one of those former cross fitters who beat the crap out of myself and am on the mend. I am truly the classic case. I thus was attracted to Dr. Kruse's interview with Ben Greenfield they did on Ben's show.

I listened to certain sections of the interview over and over. Jack was talking of the 24-36 month window to get into the PPP. I felt all of us athletes including Ben thinking, "What? Wait! Huh?, What?"

So, what's the fastest is track? If we want to get on the road to this direction, do we need to stay in ketosis all the time? Or can we have a once a week carb fest as suggested by Tim Ferris in the "Four Hour Body"? Can we have a few glasses of wine on occasion and still get "there"?

Perhaps I need to search the forum better. Maybe there is a PPP Rx blog out there. I read PPP but am looking for the RX.

I thank you all for your support. I am trying to read the blogs as fast as possible so I can catch up with everyone here. I look forward to the day when I can support others on the forum as you all have been supporting me the past week or two since I have joined.

Mel

> Began seeing keto consistently from month 3
> Adapted to keto more than to carbs ( this was a revelation I hadn't experienced before)
> Now can add carbs I couldn't before and still either be in keto or jump back to keto in 2/3 days with fasted training
> Taking few supps. But those I take very useful (Maca helps with hormonal balance, suggested by Josh... Tx Josh)
> I'm a keen cyclist so I'm doing all this alongside competition training for long distance TT
> For us girls useful to take into account month cycle esp. For CT
> Adding few carbs , gluten free, low GI has helped keto ( staying under 50g carbs)
> This is especially useful if you need functional carbs for endurance training as I do
> Will look for your journal  happy to help with any questions relating to training and Paleo or foods choices, swap of recipes I'm no tekky like these super guys but I will give it my best shot

I would be interested in knowing a little bit more regarding maca helping your hormonal balance, if you would not mind sharing this?

# Thread: Instead of Big Ass Breakfast How About Big Fat Breakfast?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-big-ass-breakfast-how-about-big-fat-breakfast.10427/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-04-02T08:21:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-big-ass-breakfast-how-about-big-fat-breakfast.10427/post-119368>

What would be wrong with making the first meal at breakfast a large fat meal, trying to prime the body for ketosis, then follow that up with protein later? Someone argue against that idea, please.

One reason for protein first might be appetite suppression, but if you are skinny person who has problems gaining weight, suppressing appetite might not be a requirement for you?

If you are prediabetic or diabetic, getting your body into ketosis early on in day might be a good way to lower the body's endogenous levels of glucose production and fight the "dawn effect" with glucose rising when you awake and keep endogenous glucose production low.

### nicld — 2014-04-02T16:22:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-big-ass-breakfast-how-about-big-fat-breakfast.10427/post-119395>

I think Jack has said that if you are not leptin resistant a big fat breakfast is great. I would add that if you have issues with fat malabsorption protein is the way to go.

### persistence — 2014-04-02T20:55:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-big-ass-breakfast-how-about-big-fat-breakfast.10427/post-119453>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=119395)
>
> I think Jack has said that if you are not leptin resistant a big fat breakfast is great. I would add that if you have issues with fat malabsorption protein is the way to go.

I may have issues with fat absorption (doctor is testing for this), but I know if I have a few hours of teas with fats added I produce ketones. Therefore I must be absorbing \*enough\* fat to get the desired effect.

I think proteins are NOT a substitute for fats. My insight is that by forcing the body to produce ketones right as you rise, that this might reset the liver's endogenous level of glucose production. Protein won't give you ketone production and - for some people - the protein apparently creates more glucose through gluconeogenesis, so it would have the opposite of the desired effect of lowering glucose levels.

Also a lot of Jack's advocacy is based on building fat-burning pathways and these require you to be in ketosis.

### nicld — 2014-04-02T22:17:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-big-ass-breakfast-how-about-big-fat-breakfast.10427/post-119475>

I have a fat malabsorption issue and was told that I need to stay away from alot of fats and concentrate on proteins until I get the issue corrected. Specifically no ketogenic diet. This is easier said than done and may be the reason why I can not get my ketosis strips to read anything but that's just my N=1.

### Shijin13 — 2014-04-02T23:53:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-big-ass-breakfast-how-about-big-fat-breakfast.10427/post-119500>

Protein is the gateway to the Pentosephosphate pathway for most people who are metabolic broken. Very few of us aren't metabolically broken. I will say that over time I've gradually transitioned to FAT being my primary fuel for breakfast. Last year I got to the point where I could barely choke down 1 egg for breakfast. but fat. SIGN ME UP. But I'd been doing the BAB for about 2yrs at that point (I'm metabolically deranged - it took me that long to get to the point where protein wasn't the solution)

That said... if you don't have the issues Kate or Nicole mention - no reason not to try it. If you find that things are going well... go back to the BAB - it helps ease the transition so you can get into the PPP. it normally takes 18-24mths to transition into the PPP

### persistence — 2014-04-03T05:25:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-big-ass-breakfast-how-about-big-fat-breakfast.10427/post-119559>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=119475)

This is easier said than done and may be the reason why I can not get my ketosis strips to read anything but that's just my N=1.

What was your doctor's strategy for "fixing" the fat absorption problem? I think a lot of doctors seem to have the attitude "eat carbs not fat, problem fixed". But of course for me that creates a different problem (high glucose).

### persistence — 2014-04-03T05:26:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-big-ass-breakfast-how-about-big-fat-breakfast.10427/post-119560>

>

Did you feel anything different when you transitioned to the PPP? What are the markers that this change is happening?

### nicld — 2014-04-03T16:01:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-big-ass-breakfast-how-about-big-fat-breakfast.10427/post-119600>

### Shijin13 — 2014-04-03T16:41:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-big-ass-breakfast-how-about-big-fat-breakfast.10427/post-119611>

> [persistence said:](/goto/post?id=119560)

for me it was the in ability to eat anything other than fat upon waking. It was also an intense desire to get in the CT tank. Its going to be different for everyone.... but remember non-native emf can knock you out...

### persistence — 2014-04-03T22:08:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-big-ass-breakfast-how-about-big-fat-breakfast.10427/post-119656>

# Thread: First Experiment Ketosis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-experiment-ketosis.10432/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-04-02T21:03:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-experiment-ketosis.10432/post-119458>

I decided to do an experiment with how easily I could go into ketosis and wanted some feedback on my results.

Yesterday I set aside about four hours before lunch and had nothing but tea with either monounsaturated fat or saturated coconut oil added. I was adding about two tablespoons per cup, so a decent amount of oil. I would guess I drank about six cups of tea over the four hours. Oh, almost forgot I also had some Hershey's Unsweetened Baking Bar chocolate, which is 100% cacao. I think there is no sugar added on that, although they do list some carbohydrate on label. It's quite sour to the taste, so I don't think there is any substantial sugar or starch.

At the end of four hours I have my ketones up to 1.6 mmol/L. I guess technically that means I am already in ketsosis at that point? I ate a lunch of fish and vegetables, no starches, and ended up peeking my ketones around 2.5 mmol/L about two hours after finishing that lunch.

What I did notice during this experiment:

1) My steady state glucose fell from the 95 to 110 level to 80 to 90.

2) I had some sense of energy leveling out and not being so dramatically up and down.

By the next morning ketones were at 0.2 mmol/L. Would it be typical to see them disappear so quickly?

I would like to do some experiments going into ketosis and out. For one, I want to test what happens to my glucose when I try to enter ketosis rapidly in morning, before eating breakfast, by drinking some tea with lots of oil. (See other thread I started for this.) Does anyone see any harm in going in and out of ketosis frequently?

### Dali Dula — 2014-04-03T00:39:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-experiment-ketosis.10432/post-119509>

Fat bomb breakfast - heat in small saucepan
1tbs each of the following:
MCT oil
Coconut oil
Ghee
Heavy whipping cream

In a big mug put two eggs (duck if you have them)
Some cinnamon and vanilla
3tbs unprocessed cocoa powder
Add boiling water to fill mug half way
Whisk up cocoa, egg, hot water
Add melted fat, whisk till frothy
I am not hungry for at least 8 hours after this.
If you want no protein at all use just egg yolk instead of whole eggs.
You could do this with coffee a la Bulletproof, but the cocoa is great and brings some resistant starch to the game.

### yewwei.tan — 2014-04-03T01:14:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-experiment-ketosis.10432/post-119511>

Generally speaking, not eating anything at all is the fastest way to naturally get into ketosis. Coconut oil will definitely boost up blood ketone levels, which is probably why you're seeing ketones go up after consuming said meals.

You've talked a lot about your glucose management issues. Do you normally see dawn phenomena? If so, that could explain the low morning ketone readings, and you should test again 4 hours after waking to see if ketones start to rise again.

Personally, I generally wake up in the 0.5-0.8mM range, eat a fat-only meal for breakfast, and then get up to 1.0-1.5mM 4 hours post waking, and then usually hover anywhere from 1.0-2.0mM all the way until dinner. If there's any eating in between, it's almost always fat or very low carb/protein. After a regular day's dinner (roughly 100g protein), I'd be back to around 0.6-0.8mM 4 hours after the meal. This is all without consumption of particularly ketogenic fats. On days that I do specifically consume any form of MCTs, afternoon readings can get above 3.0mM.

I should note that I tend to view clinically defined ketosis (>0.5mM BHB) as a leading indicator of an underlying state of high fat oxidation, rather than a be-all-end-all goal. The goal is to get your cells to be burning fat, and "going in and out of ketosis" really means switching between different energetic substrates. My opinion is that sustained fatty acid oxidation is probably something you want if you're trying to reverse disease. Ketones can act as the switch that tells your cells to burn fat, but aren't the underlying mechanism for optimal mitochondrial function as far as I know.

### persistence — 2014-04-03T05:11:58+0300

> 1tbs each of the following:
> MCT oil

>
> In a big mug put two eggs (duck if you have them)
> Some cinnamon and vanilla
> 3tbs unprocessed cocoa powder
> Add boiling water to fill mug half way
> Whisk up cocoa, egg, hot water
> Add melted fat, whisk till frothy
