### Inger — 2014-04-12T21:04:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recomendations-for-emergency-solution-for-high-emf.10520/post-121155>

I put up a tent in the garden today. the darn EMFs brings me on my knees....

I have had way too much stress and computer time lately... and now I can start to feel the effects. I have has weird occasional "headaches" lately, almost not noticeable but enough to make it painful to jump on the trampoline... not constantly but so often it starts to be a huge warning sign to me. Also I can feel I am dehydrated, I easily drink 4 liters of water... I could drink more
my shoulder joints are at times hurting a bit too in the night when I sleep on the side
i got my period again after only 10 days

So now action is required!
I have arranged my stuff to cut the stress down, and computer time too... and now - in one hour - I am going down to sleep in the garden right by the river. I raised my tent on a place I imagine have the least EMF... it is aweseome! a bit down in a "tiny valley" or kind of a pit surrounded by trees - all I can hear there is the waterfalls
so exited how I am going to sleep there. I have been thinking all the way, if I get any bad effects from the EMF I get daily, I will go and sleep in the a tent or in the deep basement. Basement is also super, just I cannot here the birds or river there... no windows. That is why I prefer the tent, lets see how it works out

Kovita maybe you could sleep somewhere in the nature too? Just sleep there? In a tent? With your hubs and child? That could be cosy
I have to do it all alone and it is a little scary as here are rats... and raccoons.. I sometimes here screams in the night from my window  but I now have no choice.

I can tell for sure now too, those man made EMFs are REALLY BAD for us

I went down into the deepest basement today and put my hands and head/face on the rocks there... (one wall is just rocks) stayed there for a while, and man it felt so good... like my body craved it!

This is not even in the city...... no wifi signals at all... neighbors all have big properties... but there are towers, they are every where. And smart meters too.

I just had a mackerel head smoothie with some wakame in it and 4 big fat oysters shucked by me for dinner....

I am just so glad I know what to do - and that it does not cost anything at all

Need to confirm my understand the damping dB..are they sufficient for sleep protection?

Just my 2 cent worth..at least this protection at night gives one some time to organise whatever it is they need to do.!
Just don't want to waste my resources

Understand where you're coming from Kovita..have a granddaughter 3yo with a redox of 65

Just remembered that Dr Bonlie recommended that the bed canopy NOT TOUCH the magnetico mattress...just in case you have one....so that instantly creates a few more deft touches to manage!

# Thread: Thinking of purchasing a Magnetico Pad...a few ?s
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-purchasing-a-magnetico-pad-a-few-s.10524/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nash14 — 2014-04-12T23:05:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-purchasing-a-magnetico-pad-a-few-s.10524/post-121167>

Hi everyone,

I'm new here, but have followed Dr. Kruse's work for a little while now. I'm about to invest in a Magnetico 20 gauss set, but I have a couple questions and concerns first. Any help would be much appreciated.

I have a used Magnetico lined up to be picked up, but it will be about a 5 hour drive back to where I live. I don't have a pickup truck, just a sedan with folding seats in the back. I'm wondering if it would be safe to lug these in my back seat, or if there are dangers and/or energy draining that's involved with being that close to any positive fields?? I don't want to set myself for a sticky situation there.

My next question is, I live in a two story townhome. On the second floor is my bedroom. Underneath on the first floor is the dining room. My concern is, wouldn't the magnetico pads on the 2nd floor cause harmful positive magnetic fields in the dining room? The ceilings are a good 8 feet high, so I'm not sure how far the positive field would go, but this was a concern.

Again any insight is much appreciated, thanks for your time!

Nash

### nicld — 2014-04-13T00:27:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-purchasing-a-magnetico-pad-a-few-s.10524/post-121170>

Not sure about the second question but I think that the 5 hours that would be spent with it in the back seat would be trivial to the benefit you will get from having one to sleep on for the next upteen years in a townhome that might be filled with EMF's.

I drove with just a twin one in my trunk for a couple of hours and did not notice anything.

### nash14 — 2014-04-13T01:07:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-purchasing-a-magnetico-pad-a-few-s.10524/post-121173>

Thank-you very much nicid, that's helpful to know. I figured it probably wouldn't be a big issue, but just remembered Dr. Bonlie mentioning in a podcast how they tested positive fields on patients and they felt quite ill. I believe the gauss amount was much higher though.

One other question I forgot to ask, would using a grounding sheet also be recommended with the magnetico? I've used the grounding sheet in the past, but have not done so since we moved into our apartment due to concern that grounding with lots of wifi around might only cause more harm than good.

### Gracelind — 2014-04-13T02:07:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-purchasing-a-magnetico-pad-a-few-s.10524/post-121186>

My two 10 gauss pads arrived rolled up in two cardboard boxes. Other than having to lift two 92 lb loads, the delivery guy seemed okay. ;-)

These are twins, so presumably carry less overall magnetic force.

Only concern is for my BIL who wears an internal defibrillator--he needs to stay away from that bedroom.

### nicld — 2014-04-13T02:53:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-purchasing-a-magnetico-pad-a-few-s.10524/post-121193>

I would not use the grounding sheets with it, it would do more harm then good I think.

### seanb4 — 2014-04-14T12:26:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-purchasing-a-magnetico-pad-a-few-s.10524/post-121412>

Grounding sheets can pick up EMFs like an antenna if you live in a high EMF area. I think grounding straps are better but I would start with just the magnetico first then add the grounding in later.

I don't think the 5hr drive is much concern TBH. You'll be getting many more hrs in the negative field to more than balance it out.

I think the field is 4ft all around the 20 gauss pad. So if you put it on a bed frame as well it will probably only extend to 2ft below your celling. So as long as your not really tall and spend a lot of time in the kitchen I don't think it will be a problem. You might want to email magnetico and ask though.

### Terminator — 2014-04-14T18:02:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-purchasing-a-magnetico-pad-a-few-s.10524/post-121437>

> [nash14 said:](/goto/post?id=121167)
>
> Hi everyone,
>
> I'm new here, but have followed Dr. Kruse's work for a little while now. I'm about to invest in a Magnetico 20 gauss set, but I have a couple questions and concerns first. Any help would be much appreciated.
>
> I have a used Magnetico lined up to be picked up, but it will be about a 5 hour drive back to where I live. I don't have a pickup truck, just a sedan with folding seats in the back. I'm wondering if it would be safe to lug these in my back seat, or if there are dangers and/or energy draining that's involved with being that close to any positive fields?? I don't want to set myself for a sticky situation there.
>
> My next question is, I live in a two story townhome. On the second floor is my bedroom. Underneath on the first floor is the dining room. My concern is, wouldn't the magnetico pads on the 2nd floor cause harmful positive magnetic fields in the dining room? The ceilings are a good 8 feet high, so I'm not sure how far the positive field would go, but this was a concern.
>
> Again any insight is much appreciated, thanks for your time!
>
> Nash

I had a similar situation and concern and spoke to a Dr. Bonlie's wife on the phone. She told me that I should only be concerned if I spend too much time in it's negative field. Spending short periods of time daily in their negative field wont hurt you.

I bought an used single core to test and was sold quickly. I even tested body voltage and it does increase natural body voltage which is very critical.

My 7 year old daughter slept on my bed few nights. After that she begged my wife to buy her magnetico pads without knowing anything about what they do to you. She sensed that they do something special. Now, all my family has them.

Get them, you won't be disappointed !

### nonchalant — 2014-04-15T18:21:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-purchasing-a-magnetico-pad-a-few-s.10524/post-121665>

A new Magnetico pad is delivered with its negative polarity directed outwards. A used pad would probably just be rolled up while on the bed, which would put its south pole on the outside.

### Andrea — 2014-04-15T18:31:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-purchasing-a-magnetico-pad-a-few-s.10524/post-121670>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=121665)
>
> A new Magnetico pad is delivered with its negative polarity directed outwards. A used pad would probably just be rolled up while on the bed, which would put its south pole on the outside.

Actually, the sleep pads are rolled with the magnets on the outside. So the core (bottom) pad has one pole outward, and the booster (top) pad has the other pole outward. When rolling the pads for transport or whatever reason, the bottom one needs to be flipped over before rolling.
If they are rolled with the magnets on the inside, the pads may be damaged beyond repair.

### nonchalant — 2014-04-15T20:03:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-purchasing-a-magnetico-pad-a-few-s.10524/post-121693>

Thanks, Rerun, for setting me right!

[http://www.abc.net.au/news/2014-04-...with-homeopathy3a-peak-research-counc/5377886](http://www.abc.net.au/news/2014-04-09/australians-wasting-time-with-homeopathy3a-peak-research-counc/5377886)

I havent had any time as yet to explore the nuances of Beneviste's work, however the Coherence models that Pollack speaks of appear to show some hope of explaining how homeopathy works.
The story sparked a massibe twitter storm, and we seemed to have had many mainstream docs irate, Jack you even got to witness one cancer researcher's ongoing tirade (@whereisdaz) who has gone on to spread crap about your work, which im sure is like water off a duck's back, as it should be.

i forget the name of the "law" in medicine, but it states that the smallest physiological stimuli result in the largest changes...

any ideas or thoughts?

# Thread: My crazy ideas
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-crazy-ideas.10528/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 109
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Albert83BCN — 2014-04-13T16:18:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-crazy-ideas.10528/post-121265>

Ok, it has been several weeks that I had the idea to start a thread here with some ideas that hit my brain from time to time, most of them triggered by the principles laid out in this forum.

I'd love to be able to post on Ask Jack to get his ideas from all these but, since I can't, I guess I can open a thread here and let him or whoever might want to, to enter and chime in

Today I was doing quite an off-topic on the [Quorum Sensing and Human Microbiota thread](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/quorum-sensing-and-human-microbiota.9682/page-2#post-121245) so I thought it could be perfect timing to open this new one. I guess I could bring new life to my optimal journey one but, come to think of it, this is not about me, but about everything!

Well, the idea is to spit out some random musings. Just some minutes ago I had this one:

"Could hate be a survival reaction when you sense that someone else is stealing electrons from you at a very quick rate?"

This is just a title I came up right now but it could be formulated in a lot of ways. Let me try to explain (please remember I'm a spaniard and my english sucks big time, I don't even know how to put my ideas in my mind in Spanish, so you bet in English...). How are feelings related to our redox potential? When I was a child I had a pretty kind nature, but as I grow older, I feel like I'm less tolerant to others actions and thinking, and I observed the rest of the world seems to behave in a similar fashion. I think that explains why relationships grew harder and harder as we get older. N0w, considering that we are born to start a journey to death, and all things related to longevity, the redox potential, etc... I came to think: most people would tell we are just more concerned with our duties as we get older, we have more responsabilities, more burdens, etc... but... if you think about it, every single moment in your timeline seems an important one, when I was a child some silly things still meant a world to me, and failing at them would not bring the kind of distrust I get nowadays.

So I think: maybe as our redox potential makes the difference, and as it gets lower and lower (poor diet, sedentary life, obesity, inflammation, circadian mismatch, messed up environtment with non-native EMF, neolithic diseases, etc..), according to what Jack said, we leak more electrons to our environtment.... he always puts it on the topic about nutrition and health but... I bet my ass it expands to every single area in life. I'm quite sure this has been discused at some place but the only one I came up to (the Redox RX) I can't recall reading about such things as laughing, mood, trust... at least, I can recall hearing Jack talking about love in some of his podcasts as a guest.

Ok where I'm trying to get to? Today I was in the kitchen with my mom. This recently I must admit I cannot bear with her at some times. She's the kind of person that gets concerned by the most stupid things in life ever. A simple thing like keeping the fridge open for more than 5 seconds can start a discussion at home. My dad also thinks the same way. It seems as if everything we can do is wrong and she never empowers us to do what we feel comfortable to, just what she perceives as OK even if it does not make any sense to us... So, today, when I felt like constantly observed as I was preparing some Brussels sprouts with pumpkin and a big egg with oregano and garlic powder topped with guacamole, I got to think 'heck, I'm starting to hate you!'

Don't get me wrong, I love my mum, and she has and has ever had that kindred nature that a son is expected to get from his mother, she's probably the most important person to me in this world, but the way she handles things... I started considering that is hurting me, not empowering me to unleash my potential. I have to deal with quite a bunch of things at this moment and that stupid things she obligates me to focus on instead the ones I should be focusing... I kind of feel 'threatened' when I'm near her. I feel like it's best to me to keep some distance If I want to find the inner peace I need to focus on my things. So then I started thinking about the redox potential and relationships, and tried to tie things. In fact, we have heard for ages about the ideas of 'people's energy' and how some people can drain your energy and how other can empower you. Positive and negative thinking and all that stuff, you know.

What I wonder: In a deeper level, is it tied at all at the ability to transfer electrons from one person to another? When we get to love someone... is it because we get a positive influx of electrons that increase our redox potential? Do we get attached to it at a higher level with that thing we know as oxytocin and dopamine? And if things go wrong... may it be a sudden change in polarity? Do we pass from love to hate because of this? And when we detect that a huge loss of electrons is happening or it is going to happen... is when we try to get rid of it? Is this what we know as cortisol? And if we keep unable to get away from this situation... is it what it keeps us from being well? ### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-13T16:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-crazy-ideas.10528/post-121272>

I just posted my response to you in the other thread...........
Albert your intuition seems to both shock and delight you. Why? Because your are beginning to see how the quantum mechanism begins so small and can end up in massive complexity. Let me give you another example from my book. You might have missed it. How does glaciation occur?

It is now generally believed that ice ages are initiated by small changes in the earth’s orbit, caused by, among other things, the gravitational tug of Jupiter and Saturn. These incredibly small changes in the natural laws of physics alter the distribution of sunlight (electromagnetic force) across different latitudes at different times of year. When the amount of light hitting the far northern latitudes in summer approaches a minimum, snow begins to build up there. This initiates a feedback cycle that causes atmospheric carbon dioxide levels to drop. Temperatures fall, which leads more ice to build up, and so on. After a while, the orbital cycle enters a new phase, and the feedback loop begins to run in reverse. The ice starts to melt, global CO2 levels rise, and the ice melts back farther.

I want to remind you what I said about gravity and the electromagnetic force...........they both have infinite range. They differ in their power. The electromagnetic force is much stronger.....................yet, I just showed you how a small tug of gravity from two other planets can alter the world you evolved from in massive fashion.

Humans are the universe in miniature, almost a caricature of nature’s full complexity.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-13T16:51:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-crazy-ideas.10528/post-121274>

> [Albert83BCN said:](/goto/post?id=121265)
>
> In fact, we have heard for ages about the ideas of 'people's energy' and how some people can drain your energy and how other can empower you. Positive and negative thinking and all that stuff, you know.

Yes, but we also hear it rather round the other way -- and sometimes from the same people. I'll give an example:

Epictetus:

> The key is to keep company only with people who uplift you, whose presence calls forth your best.

Yet the same writer says:

> When you are offended at any man's fault, turn to yourself and study your own failings. Then you will forget your anger.

Those are actually the top two quotations on this page!

[http://www.brainyquote.com/quotes/authors/e/epictetus.html](http://www.brainyquote.com/quotes/authors/e/epictetus.html)

The two quotations are not necessarily at odds with each other. But if we forget the second, I guess we're in danger of always thinking it's the other person at fault. Epictetus -- and other stoic philosophers -- were very keen to say:

> It's not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.

The thought here is that you don't have, for example, to get angry. Seneca -- one of your countrymen, I think -- says much the same and has an interesting "letter" just called _De Ira_ -- On Anger. The Stoics are fairly absolute about this. The tradition coming from Aristotle says rather that one should be angry to the right amount, on the right occasion, with the right person, etc. -- the key there is "appropriateness" not trying to tear the emotion up by the roots. (The view they take in the East, the Buddhist view, seems pretty much the same as the Stoic one.)

I think perhaps we should avoid people who are downright unpleasant or immoral even if their company is pleasing to us. But I think that people with whom we have to get on, because our lives are twined with theirs or we work with them or whatever -- well, we have to bear with them. I know it's hard: I can be irascible.

I think this is one of the ways where ideas for "reducing stress" (listening to music, meditation or prayer, walking in the woods, biofeedback, neurofeedback, etc.) come in. If we're calm and in a happy mood, then things appear different to us.

I am waiting until you finally get what I have been seeing for a decade now. ### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-13T16:56:01+0300

Good results are difficult when indifference predominates. You must seek your answer. When we are seeking our answer, we cannot be indifferent about the questions we ask, because we want the answer that brings transformation. So, the first questions that lead to the right answers we must ask of ourselves: Am I making a positive deposit into my future? What will I reap from this decision? What is motivating me to take this action? When we ask ourselves the right questions we will lead ourselves to the right answers. The problem is not to find the answer, it's to face the answer. Begin to live with the answer than die with the question.

Is your perception reality? Only a quiet mind perceives the world''s reality. Sometimes our perception is not reality because we look through the eyes of hurt, pain, anger, envy, the unseen. We are enslaved by anything we do not consciously see. But we are freed by our conscious perception. What we all have to learn is, that we're responsible for our perception of things. As we gain wisdom, we see the magic in things because our senses grow sharper. Reality is that which, when we stop believing in it, doesn't go away.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-13T16:56:51+0300

Science says momentum is a conserved quantity unless affected by external forces.

In life we can enhance or preserve our momentum by deciding what force we will expose our mind too. Will we be exposing our mind to positive forces , similar or higher spirited individuals to increase our momentum or to negative people or negative news , unfruitful activity to decrease the momentum.

Momentum builds up when you invest in people and build leaders. When you raise leaders you multiply, when you win followers you have addition.

Monuments are build to glorify your achievements and it tell people great you used to be.

Don't stop if you are moving forward. Don't have limited vision that involves success only for yourself. Don't settle down in this life by being satisfied with just a Monument .

Momentum shows that your have a greater vision, you are investing in building people, you are not easily satisfied, you keep challenging yourself to push higher.

Physics defines momentum as a product of mass and velocity.

In life, momentum is a product of inner vision and daily actions; what sets difference in momentum or our inner drive between different people is how they envision their goals, envision themselves and do something about it.

MINDSET:

Reality is what I take to be true.

What I take to be true is what I believe.

What I believe is based upon My perceptions.

What I perceive depends upon what I look for.

What I look for depends upon what I think.

What I think depends upon what I perceive.

What I perceive determines what I believe.

What I believe determines what I take to be true.

What I take to be true is My reality.

> [Albert83BCN said:](/goto/post?id=121265)
>
> Ok, it has been several weeks that I had the idea to start a thread here with some ideas that hit my brain from time to time, most of them triggered by the principles laid out in this forum.
>
> I'd love to be able to post on Ask Jack to get his ideas from all these but, since I can't, I guess I can open a thread here and let him or whoever might want to, to enter and chime in
>
> Today I was doing quite an off-topic on the [Quorum Sensing and Human Microbiota thread](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/quorum-sensing-and-human-microbiota.9682/page-2#post-121245) so I thought it could be perfect timing to open this new one. I guess I could bring new life to my optimal journey one but, come to think of it, this is not about me, but about everything!
>
> Well, the idea is to spit out some random musings. Just some minutes ago I had this one:
>
> "Could hate be a survival reaction when you sense that someone else is stealing electrons from you at a very quick rate?"
>
> This is just a title I came up right now but it could be formulated in a lot of ways. Let me try to explain (please remember I'm a spaniard and my english sucks big time, I don't even know how to put my ideas in my mind in Spanish, so you bet in English...). How are feelings related to our redox potential? When I was a child I had a pretty kind nature, but as I grow older, I feel like I'm less tolerant to others actions and thinking, and I observed the rest of the world seems to behave in a similar fashion. I think that explains why relationships grew harder and harder as we get older. N0w, considering that we are born to start a journey to death, and all things related to longevity, the redox potential, etc... I came to think: most people would tell we are just more concerned with our duties as we get older, we have more responsabilities, more burdens, etc... but... if you think about it, every single moment in your timeline seems an important one, when I was a child some silly things still meant a world to me, and failing at them would not bring the kind of distrust I get nowadays.
>
> So I think: maybe as our redox potential makes the difference, and as it gets lower and lower (poor diet, sedentary life, obesity, inflammation, circadian mismatch, messed up environtment with non-native EMF, neolithic diseases, etc..), according to what Jack said, we leak more electrons to our environtment.... he always puts it on the topic about nutrition and health but... I bet my ass it expands to every single area in life. I'm quite sure this has been discused at some place but the only one I came up to (the Redox RX) I can't recall reading about such things as laughing, mood, trust... at least, I can recall hearing Jack talking about love in some of his podcasts as a guest.
>
> Ok where I'm trying to get to? Today I was in the kitchen with my mom. This recently I must admit I cannot bear with her at some times. She's the kind of person that gets concerned by the most stupid things in life ever. A simple thing like keeping the fridge open for more than 5 seconds can start a discussion at home. My dad also thinks the same way. It seems as if everything we can do is wrong and she never empowers us to do what we feel comfortable to, just what she perceives as OK even if it does not make any sense to us... So, today, when I felt like constantly observed as I was preparing some Brussels sprouts with pumpkin and a big egg with oregano and garlic powder topped with guacamole, I got to think 'heck, I'm starting to hate you!'
>
> Don't get me wrong, I love my mum, and she has and has ever had that kindred nature that a son is expected to get from his mother, she's probably the most important person to me in this world, but the way she handles things... I started considering that is hurting me, not empowering me to unleash my potential. I have to deal with quite a bunch of things at this moment and that stupid things she obligates me to focus on instead the ones I should be focusing... I kind of feel 'threatened' when I'm near her. I feel like it's best to me to keep some distance If I want to find the inner peace I need to focus on my things. So then I started thinking about the redox potential and relationships, and tried to tie things. In fact, we have heard for ages about the ideas of 'people's energy' and how some people can drain your energy and how other can empower you. Positive and negative thinking and all that stuff, you know.
>
> What I wonder: In a deeper level, is it tied at all at the ability to transfer electrons from one person to another? When we get to love someone... is it because we get a positive influx of electrons that increase our redox potential? Do we get attached to it at a higher level with that thing we know as oxytocin and dopamine? And if things go wrong... may it be a sudden change in polarity? Do we pass from love to hate because of this? And when we detect that a huge loss of electrons is happening or it is going to happen... is when we try to get rid of it? Is this what we know as cortisol? And if we keep unable to get away from this situation... is it what it keeps us from being well? When we get anxious and binge eat because we feel relieve... are we trying to deal with that massive loss of electrons by a higher intake of quick acting food (say carbs?). In fact... what is in reality anxiety? Might it just be sudden change in the entropy state of our system that does not seem to be able to get balanced with small things and that then manifestates as this kind of uneasy feeling we got to know as anxiety? Do we only get relaxed and comfortable again once things get again to a quantum equilibrium according to what we can sense as equilibrium? And to find this equilibrium and achieve well being (high serotonin?) is it more and more difficult as time goes by and we get messed more and more due to our current environtment? Do we loose our ability to feel well and then become another part of the system that steals electrons to some electron donors as our redox potential goes down and down in order to balance the equation? Is it mood that tied to the redox potential?

i love your ideas and the way you think albert
it is very congruent to the way i think

i see the electron transfer in relationships all the time
where one must be strong when the other is weak, the one who becomes weak stays functional borrowing from the other's charge
this is another reason the saying "opposites attract" is true in relationships, and why when one who meets another who is too alike, they often butt heads.

often it is seen in elderly couples who have lived in a synergistic relationship for many many years.
one dies, and the next day the spouse dies. as if their flame were extinguished.
a break up might spell a sudden loss in charge, as you feel what you have donated has been whisked away, before a chance to gain reciprocal electrons...

finding people who fuel and balances your energy is paramount to a good friend set.

Absolutely, this has puzzled me for some time also, how the heck people that get it so far, when one goes away the other one follows so fast... and it must be tied to all this for sure. Interesting what you say about break ups, several days ago I was thinking exactly the same! When I was younger, between 17-25 I used to get very very down when a relationship ended, I couldn't handle it at all. My entire world would just collapse until I would find a new one... I was the kind of person who felt quite lonely and incomplete without a partner, I also had a great tendency to feeling bad and to be insecure about myself... as I boy I'd say I had more the mindset of a girl than actually the one of the boy. I was a little bit obese also by that time and only ate crap. In a sense, I think that it's no wonder why many of that relationships ended, I think I was a draining source of electrons to my partners back then. They felt it and left. Today I think that the exact opposite happens. Right now I could care no less about such things, I made myself the center of my life, I've discovered many fascinating things that keep my mind spinning and changed my perception of life... I also took major changes at some point like caring about my nutrition and fitness that changed the direction about what I was going to become and what I have become... and now I feel like I'm the electron source to my partner, not the opposite. And I don't live in fear that my partner will leave me anymore because I just know it won't happen, I can sense how positive I am to her, but I'm also not comfortable with her being like that, because I can see she's at the point I once were. That's why I keep hurting her head a little bit and try to push her boundaries even if I think some of the things I might do or tell her will hurt her short term, I want her to become another source of electrons for the people that surrounds her. And I think that in this last 3 years It kind of happened, but we have to step up our game. Certainly being aware of all this knowledge we share here makes it easier to grasp why things happen...

### cmegan — 2014-04-14T16:37:20+0300

Albert I think your posting is right on target, and I think many people historically refer to this phenomena as someone being an "energy drainer"...but looking at it from the perspective of electron transfer is much more accurate and interesting! And as you get healthier you become much more sensitive to this and may find it more difficult to be in certain situations and around certain people. Yes, I agree, it is very important to find friends and lovers who can fuel and balance your energies. With family members who drain us we just need to be aware of how to keep balanced as best as we can when around them and try to stay heart centered. I work as a medical psychologist and can really feel the "drain" after sitting with certain patients after 50 minutes and need to keep myself grounded. I used to be both in private practice and in a hospital based practice and now find that I can only do private practice because I have become so much more sensitive and my environment needs to be balanced and serene. Funny though, and I'm sure other clinicians can relate to this, but it wasn't the patients in the hospital based practice that were draining me, it was my colleagues and all of the drama and politics!

This is a trauma, a hard hit... When you as a living unit do not have enough energy in the system small changes, big changes do radical things to you. Today we loose so much energy that the smallest thing starts the tsunami of illness inside of us, see this every time i work.

It can start with a direct hit to your arm, your energy balance a direct healing but in the same moment you must take from something else. The most delicate and sublime things is what goes first, survival is your first choice BUT what next? You can not heal the "wound" and the steal syndrome gets worse... In short time a chain of reactions starts depending on your background. If you do not get the energy transfer to work everything goes haywire... Stiffens up and dies.

This is happening everywhere today... In familys, in illness, in business and mankind.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-04-15T15:16:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-crazy-ideas.10528/post-121624>

Are you impliying that I'm dangerous because I think too much? Well, maybe

Let's see how dangerous may I be. Today I was assembling a blogpost with [my latest reviewed scientific studies](http://t.co/ukx5K3aw5n) and had this crazy idea: Assuming the microbiome has a deep impact on our health and behaviour (see the '[Quorum Sensing and Human Microbiota](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/quorum-sensing-and-human-microbiota.9682/)' thread) and certain things there are well established as of today, like that serotonin is made in the gut, that the gut health can impact serotonin levels and our wellness via the gut-brain axis and so on... lets try to tie it with a quite different topic. I was reading about a paper on meditation and how it can change our brain function. We all know about the touted benefits of meditation. I was thinking... could it be that in the same fashion that we, as humans, from time to time when things go wrong we can sense the 'call of the earth' and do some things to try to fix our acts that destroy the planet... when we meditate we're calling our gut microbiome and body cells to hear about our thoughts and exert a signal mechanism that might ressonate in them so they can change the epigenetic signalling that ultimately directs our actions and health effects? This thinking emerged with the comments on that thread about the worlds within worlds from the book 'Horton Hears a Who', it really made my head start spinning around.

PD: Jack, I know you'll ask me to read the lastest [blogpost](https://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-1-gutcollagen-link/). I'm 50% the way, and [you were right](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/quorum-sensing-and-human-microbiota.9682/page-2#post-119096). I'm loving it!

### Albert83BCN — 2014-04-16T13:48:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-crazy-ideas.10528/post-121809>

Today I remembered about another really crazy idea I had some time ago. The thing is that I considered getting the 23andme test. It's not that I'm after some issue in particular, but being into nutrition and health, an student, N=1'r and a geeky mind... I guess it's no wonder my curiosity would find its way into something like this.

Then, when I researched a little bit about it, I saw that 23andme co-founder is (was) Go0-gl3 co-founder wife... Seems like a weird coincidence to me? Lets consider this:

-   Gug1' knows everything about us, first from their browser: they know what you search, that means they know what bothers you, what do you like, what do you hate, fear, what do you research, what do you want to know about... they know you. Depending on how many years you've been using it (10+ for me maybe?) they have almost all your behaviour serialized, and probably almost a hint of your soul.
-   Now, with GUg le+, Maps and Android mobile devices, they also know about all your agenda, where you are in the World at most of the time, who do you engage with... Even if you were to hide it, they'd probably know via some of your relatives.
-   And now, with your DNA, they know exactly what is your genetic blueprint.

Now, considering that tissue engineering seems feasible AFAIK... is it crazy to think that, err... maybe G's ultimate goal might be to be able to clone anyone of us?

Ok, now if I do not reply in a few days, I hope you'll know where to start looking for me

They said in my simple version, human is longing for every kind of information, we crave to have it 24/7/365... The problem is, we tend to filter and with their vision we should have everything published ever at easy access once again 24/7/365. He also said that humans are to slow, to weak in their computing so they would provide it and we do the search, as we do that they with CPU:s calculate what we LONG TO KNOW...

For me this was perhaps a start to get out of business forever, i do not want google inside my brain, i do not want apple in my hand.

For me, this is NOT how i hoped we should use the technology and wonders it can do.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-04-26T18:55:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-crazy-ideas.10528/post-123522>

A thought can change our DNA, that initially crazy idea looks like a fact, as of today. Every day I believe in this idea more and more. Yet, commitment is the way to finally turn ideas into reality. Jack always tells us so. But, what if you have great ideas and can visualize future yet cannot still be able to put enough commitment to turn them into reality? Is that just a failure from us? How does the laws of physics and evolution intercede with us? Do they play a role with it? I can sense they do. If I set for an easy goal, like focusing on getting to sleep a few minutes earlier than I did yesterday, to improve my circadian cycle, it looks like a reasonable goal, does not need a lot of energy to be performed successfully, and in the world as I know it seems a place where statistically that future is likely candidate to become the future I will get into. But, if I set for a really high goal, like influencing the world up to a point where It will change it's complete mindset... it seems to me that such a strong change in how things are arranged cannot be turn into reality unless massive amounts of energy are invested in order to allow for it to occur. I really needs a lot of commitement and work to be able to be transfered into actionable energy that leads to that future. Maybe just too much depending on the goal. Isn't it naive for me to think that I might achieve something like that? Is it what it really means to have not realistic expectations, when someone tells you so? So... if I fail at something... is it really my fault? Or maybe it's just the laws of thermodynamics not allowing me to? And if I could gather massive amounts of energy... could I defeat the law? Did you ever thought about it Jack? I bet you did. Did you choose to raise so many starfish as a way to gather the enough power to blend the future to suit your present vision and defeat the unlikely odds to change the world of medicine by yourself? With the power of your community? A mental army? I think that's also what Bruce Lipton wanted to tell us in his talks about how systems evolve only when they become one and allow to turn into a greater level of consciousness... May it also a plan to spare electrons from yours as a plan to offset the massive drainage that would inflict such an ambitious goal if only taken by yourself? is it why we need friends and supporting people in our lifes? May it be why having great responsibilities that depend only on ourselves is so draining and hard to bear? Is it why big goals alone can be only achieved by amazing people and not the average joe? Those that can gather enough redox potential to turn them into reality? And if they push it too far... is it also why genius often die young? Or they even commit suicide? A massive electron flux lost from that could not be dealt with? Why would someone even commit suicide if not? is it low serotonin and dopamine a restriction of the universe for us to stop doing what its not meant to from the whole perspective? Or to allow the one standing next to you to do what he should be doing to? Einstein said that God does not play dices with the universe... but according to this [recent paper](<http://Statistical physics of self-replication>)... maybe just the dices arrange themselves in a certain way so we can finally awake to think that as the consequence? Does make sense if the plan of the universe as a whole evolving thing is to get rid of them because the way they were interacting with the environment was not meant to. If we see this world from the laws of thermodinamics it seems like a not so weird consideration... strange things happen everyday... as a way to reorganize the world and allow for more diversity? This all weird thinking started because today I thought that stress must be the inexorable loss of electrons from a situation that requires too much energy to just be dealt by an easy solution... a quantum physics explanation for what biochemistry says it's solved by a hormone called cortisol that makes us more alert in order to get us to react to changes in our environment. Yet, in the world we live, where we achieved so many unbelievable things, and we have tons of tecnology, and machines moving electrons at blazing fast speed... can we deal with it? Part of the power to fuel that we think they come from the resources we get from nature, electric energy and such, yet, when presented what we have seen in the latest blog series... we could think that to expect only to isolate where every single closed system gets its energy from would be too much optimistic... so I think I can understand from that perspective what Jack says about electronic devices stealing electrons from us when we use it. Yep, everything seems to make sense yet it leads to more and more questions and thoughts i don't understand at all just keep ressonating in my head, as if my head was a quantum computer than can see the ideas pass by but not capture them as in the heisenberg uncertainty principle. Well I'm not sure what I have just wrote here, I think I'll just push enter so I do not feel the temptation to delete it all and let that energy left from me and got by the PC without returning some electrons to the world they could travel to, via the net. Did I lowered or changed the entropy of the universe with that? Will it inflict massive change into the future? Will it be forgotten in the immensity of the net? Or will it turn from a 2D textual space as it's the screen into a 3D quantum machine like it's the human brain that will finally turn it into empowering energy to allow for action? The reverse process from where it came from? Will it become a source for action for a very different plan from someone else that reads it whose thoughts and plans I cannot even get to imagine? Am I experimenting with the butterfly effect? Yes I think so. Did you ever thought Jack that your forum would lead to such a weird post? LOL

Today I talked to a friend of mine who paints, I know her context, she's not what we would take for optimal... and to me it seems she is doing similar things to what Jeremy does, painting seems to sustain her, I think she tries to get from painting (she used to sculpt also) the energy she is losing to the environment with her health issues, or at least that's what came to my mind when I remembered about the quilt and from the perspective of this site. She told me that now that has a child she cannot get to do it so often and that she feels she is lacking it. CW would say she just gets distracted with that, I guess. Whatever. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-crazy-ideas.10528/post-124895>

Today I was walking by down the street and just realized that near a McDonalds that has been there for a year or so has just opened a new ready to take restaurant that claims to cook traditional and healthy food. With the new prism I look at things at this moment, I thought: "Uhmm... could it be that this new restaurant emerged just to counter the deleterious existence of the McDonalds? A predictable future driven by the laws of thermodynamics?" It even inspired a [quick post on my blog](http://www.nutricioblog.com/2014/05/03/reflexions-entropia-univers-ordre-el-mcdonalds-i-el-nostrum/) with these crazy ideas of mine

.. and a lot more... I'm engaged in too many but hope to at least to cope with two of them. That's what I do when I'm not hearing at some podcasts or surfing PubMed.

Sometimes seems weird that such many strange things at first glance keep appearing in your life in such a convenient way... doesn't it? have you ever thought that maybe you're the chosen one to full-fill something? and that the laws of the thermodynamics are cheating on the dices to allow for the future to evolve in a way that presents to you what you need to grasp it?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-06T06:03:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-crazy-ideas.10528/post-125425>

So how is this for crazy ideas............

Avogadro's constant is a scaling factor between macroscopic and microscopic ([atomic scale](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atomic_unit)) observations of nature...........

Small subatomic changes lead to wide alterations in energy. This is why the brain works on just 20 watts. It takes an electron and strips its mass and the result is a photon with no mass. So I have been trying all day to show how this effect of the brain scales in nature.........So I am invoking Avogadro's number and the problem has been bugging me for two days. The electron rest mass (symbol: me) is the mass of a stationary electron. It is one of the fundamental constants of physics, and is also very important in chemistry because of its relation to the Avogadro constant. It has a value of about 9.109... X 10 −31 kilograms or about 5.486×10−4 atomic mass units, equivalent to an energy of about 8.19×10−14 joules or about 0.511 megaelectronvolts.

The weight of one kilogram near the surface of the earth is 9.8 Newtons.

The weight of an electron would then be 9.1093829 x 10-31 \* 9.8, or ~8.9 x 10-30 Newtons.

The ball at the end of a Bic pen is ~1mm diameter tungsten carbide. At .52mm3 in volume and WC being 8.6g/cm3, we have approximately 4mg of tungsten carbide.

As of 4AM GMT, the ISS is flying at 413km above the surface of the earth.

If the ISS flew over your head right now, and you weighed 70kg, the force of the gravitational attraction between your body and the ball at the end of a ball point pen floating in the cupola would be (G \* 70kg \* .000004kg)/413000m2 , or 1.095x10-25 N, or roughly twenty thousand times greater than the weight of an electron.

FWIW, due to doppler effects in the gravity gravitational waves coming from the moving ball point pen, the force vector will be lagged behind the location of the ball by about 23m at 413km, or about .003 degrees.

It just occurred to me that since scientists just have re-estimated the electron weight figured out to 13 significant figures. If someone could actually measure the force of gravity from the aforementioned orbiting ball of tungsten carbide to just ten significant figures, you could actually determine the distance to that ball with a precision of +/-.1mm. Add one other detector and you could triangulate its position, providing you with the ability to read what the pen is writing.............while this is all going on.............assuming nothing else in the universe is moving.

And Albert thought he has crazy thoughts..............

### Albert83BCN — 2014-05-09T12:34:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-crazy-ideas.10528/post-126008>

I'm having so many crazy ideas these days that I wouldn't know where to re-take the thread. Let's do it with an idea just came though my mind just now from a comment I saw on Twitter from a certain girl:

> "Rule of thumb Twitterer ppl if I am complain being hungry and fat it's probably just mid luteal just ignore me do this all time"

Ignore it says... that advice is meaningless. The sole fact that her thoughts made it to the net is small interaction in this universe that will cause a change in some form, and the proof is that the prior tweets emerged in the form of a debate. Even the smallest insignificant thing will stablish a relationship with other things and develop a greater form of complexity... because this is how the laws of thermodynamics and entropy rule the arrow of time of our version of the universe.... at the sub-atomic level it is an electron interacting between atoms with protons and neutrons and all that stuff.... that form compounds, smaller organisms and such things in the microscopic level... that lead to the upper levels of organization of matter... up to the ecosystem where we live, with people that conforms another level of the scale in itself which is a society....

That ties with another though I have several days ago... the real crazy one. Well, It has been some time now, but nowadays I have new insight... where is the humanity head to? Unless Jack message becomes so strong that causes a great turn of events... I feel that the future of the human race, if there's not a major disaster before, is to evolve into a kind of body fat sphere that's almost fat and DHA... why?

We have created a new world of technology that lead to a very fast change in our environment that has speed up epigenetics like crazy. That's well stablished in JK posts... well, I realized (not difficult to) that we people are leading our lives toward technology in such a strong way that we do less things by moving and more from 'ordering' and interacting through tecnlogy... we talk here instead of doing it face to face (that would be crazy to think 100 years ago, now it's reality), we order food from the net to get it at home... buy things at amazon... at this rate, I don't see why we would get so proficient at it that we'll start to really get disconnected from the reality we still live in. Back in the 90's multiverses on the net started to emerge in the form of apps like [Second Life](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Second_Life). Today it seems that it has lost the initial hype but I think that this is because in fact, our real life is now more similar to that than a simulated one. That's why something initially more appealing like a game has been beaten by something more mundane like Facebook or other Social Networks in general. No need for a Second Life because we're becoming that. Probably there will be no much time since scientists start developing biotronic devices that can interact with the human body commercially rather than research stage as it is right now (I bet jack knows about this stuff as a neurosurgeon) and our epigenetics will engulf them as crazy because that's what they need to make another leap in our evolution... one that gets lost in a digital world. We're fatter now more than ever and shortened our gut and lowered our microbiota... I don't see why this cannot get expanded to unknown levels where we also loose muscle but gain fat to fuel a brain that can start merging with technology, uniting biologic semiconductors like collagen and water to the current silicon ones, or perhaps something more evolved like the carbon nanotubes they're working to deliever faster computers... to create a new form of massive reality build upon networking between human tissue and electronic devices.

Cooperation, in the end, is probably the key to emerging new forms of complex life. One of the most accepted [theories of evolution](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Endosymbiotic_theory) suggests that more complex cells like eukaryotic ones emerged from the symbiosis of more basic cells when a certain change happened that the one that merged with the other gave life to a new entity in itself... This all tied altogether sounds a lot like the Matix movie but if you think about it to me it makes sense...

We have already gained more and more efficiency interacting with technology over time, first big keyboards with text in a black screen, then the mouse with colour windows representations, then the miniaturization of the devices and tactile technology and the internet.... every step allows for more complex interaction that brings new applications like the one I'm just using to establish this communication with all of you. The next step will for sure bring even higher complexity of behaviour... up to the point where what I stated seems possible.

Man, I should consider starting making a life from selling plots for sci-fi movies and TV shows

### Albert83BCN — 2014-05-09T15:48:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-crazy-ideas.10528/post-126018>

Uhm... that's very interesting Josh! I pictured a slightly different view of the process. I imagine our current universe as a rounded particle that we could say it's discrete to make things simpler but that if you zoom in codifies an entire universe. When it is discrete the entropy is zero. Each time you make an infinitesimal step to look closer, your picking an arrow of time, that conforms a direction forward in time and conforms a new future. You then just stepped into another universe in itself. If you were to took the step into a different direction from that sphere, you'd get a different version of the universe. Each time you take a step forward you develop the future, as if you were playing an old music box. If you take step 1 into one direction and the closest possible step 2 that will lead into the closest universe you could ever imagine, you made two new spheres in itself. If you look at both to create a new one, the entropy is higher in the system. Yet, if you again look at one step in isolation, you get another 0 entropy system as a dot, that will have infinite possibilities to unfold again, and again and again.... that means my vision of the universe is something like we are and endlessly conformation of spheres that overlap with the closest possible universe and that are very far appart from the least probably one. A universe where I fail to write a word ok (like thiz) or I do it correctly (like THIS) is very close each one, but one universe where in fact I'm Jack writting this entry and he is me awaiting for OSF3 is more unlikely. Why? because there has been too many steps in between that would need to be reversed. A universe where a dinosaur is writting this post and I'm his food is even less feasible, yet, some of the theories point that even that universe is real somewhere.... if you picked a certain thread of events. If the whole is really infinite, then it must be true that no matter how weird an scenario is, it must be contained in the whole.

I'm still starting to read about the current threories of the universe and I just have a glimpse on it but its my understanding that the M-Theory model says that there are certain explanations that cannot be united for the whole model of the universe but that can be used to explain different scenarios, like the mapa mundi, which you cannot fully use to model the rounded Earth, but you can write down some pieces of a mapa mundi that represent the whole world as we know it if you stitch all parts of the continents. You go from a 3D to a 2D model... that ties with the perception of reality. Its probable that both the vision you gave me of the infinite tunnel folding inwards is a feasible and compatible different version of the vision I gave to you of infinite spheres...

Wow, I just cannot believe I've written this

I've got a friend, a girl, now she's 32, when we were at the University she was both enrolled in computer engineering and in music education at the same time. She was always under high stress trying to cope with both things plus other issues with her family and friends, etc. Several years ago she started having some pain on the hip that was finally diagnosed as a trochanteral bursitis and lasted for more than two years, and just when she got able to get rid of it she started getting a lot of other painful symptoms everywhere from which she has finally been diagnosed with fibromyalgia. Nowadays she can barely walk and has to get to a wheelchair if she wants to leave home for more than a few minutes. She always have had a lot of digestive issues, and last time I saw her I tried to put everything together and asked for all her issues. "_One can have different degrees (order) of quantum coherence (see The Rainbow Worm). The fully quantum coherent state would be quantum coherence of n order, n being a very large number approaching infinity. This state is only reach rarely, perhaps once or twice in a life time, for some of us, or maybe not at all. You get an inkling of it when you have an aesthetic experience, a very special aesthetic experience, like one I had when first encountering the rainbow worm. Some people would call it a mystical experience. (I’m not a mystic or a religious person, but I do love art. I do art as much as I do science, and in much the same way. That’s what makes life fulfilling for me.)

You can have lower degrees of coherence; the work-a-day coherence that keeps life ticking over. If you have a fully quantum coherent system, you will never age and you will never die. But, we do age and we do die. That’s because of incoherence of varying degrees. In my book, I suggest that time is really the accumulation of incoherence._

_When you accumulate incoherence, you age. So, I think a happy coherent person ages more slowly than someone full of angst and strive. It’s fascinating to think about that._"

[ISIS Report 23/60/10 Interview with Dr.Mae-Wan Ho](http://www.i-sis.org.uk/quantumJazzBiology.php)

# Thread: Leafy greens
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leafy-greens.10530/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-04-13T18:43:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leafy-greens.10530/post-121306>

What to do with these …

I usually just steam or boil various things of this sort briefly -- or simply them "wilt" them by cooking them in a covered pot with very little water and a knob of butter.

However, perhaps it's worth doing a bit more with them. Also perhaps it would be good to cook them longer sometimes. I think there's been quite a vogue for cooking all vegetables very briefly, people muttering "vitamins" darkly while they do so. The texture is supposed to be superior with brief cooking, too. Some people even serve stuff that's no so much _al dente_ (as the phrase has it) as rock hard.

Terry Wahls follows this preserve-the-heat-labile-nutrients thinking and would rather we eat vegetables raw or very lightly cooked. However, I think I recall Sally Fallon saying somewhere that there's a reason why people in traditional cultures cooked some vegetables for a long time. (I think it was neutralising goitrogens that she was getting at.) Don't think you know better than all those old grandmas; you don't always, as the WAPF might say. And there's the Richard Wrangham point that cooking helps to break down foods and make some of the nutrients more easily available to us:

[http://www.amazon.com/Catching-Fire-Cooking-Made-Human/dp/184668286X/](http://www.amazon.com/Catching-Fire-Cooking-Made-Human/dp/184668286X/)

I do enjoy fairly lightly cooked vegetables, but why not longer-cooked sometimes? I did, in fact, used to enjoy a Delia Smith recipe for "Braised red cabbage with apples" which she recommends with sausages or cassoulet. I haven't cooked it for ages, though.

So here's a recipe for Southern U.S. style greens I found on a paleo site. Here they're stewed with pork --
a way of flavouring it (and perhaps, originally, of stretching the meat?). I did wonder, the South being heavily settled by Scots and Scotch-Irish people, if there was a Scottish origin to this dish, old Scotland being well-known for its Kailyards. But I guess some such dishes were probably common all over Europe. The Scots, I guess, would have used mutton rather than pork.

Here it is:

[http://www.liveto110.com/southern-collard-greens-recipe/](http://www.liveto110.com/southern-collard-greens-recipe/)

I used a bag of "spring greens" that looked pretty much like the leafy green vegetable she's using here. The bag was 200g, so I needed only about a quarter of everything else. I had some cold sausages -- Toulouse ones I'd cooked the day before, so I chopped one of those up and stirred it in at the end instead of the bacon. I used butter rather olive oil and didn't add any red pepper. Otherwise I followed the recipe. It was pretty good. I'm afraid I ate it all.

### Lahelada — 2014-04-13T18:49:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leafy-greens.10530/post-121310>

Good question.I still remember tge horrified face of my host family in England who askwd me to cook cabbage.I brought out the biggest pot in the house to give it a good boil. Indeed,vitamins they muttered and We are doctors we know..."

### nonchalant — 2014-04-15T18:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leafy-greens.10530/post-121676>

Yesterday I was giving some freshly cut young poke salat its first boiling, to just wilt the leaves, and was gratified at the strong asparagus scent it gave off. I drained the water, and would have boiled it a bit more to finish cooking it, but something came up, and I had to store it in the fridge. I've been waiting a year to taste this delicious green! Yes, I'm an asparagus fan.  I certainly wouldn't eat it raw, though I admit I haven't tried it. Not willing to risk the tummy drama. Tonight I'll see how it tastes after being stored.

DH knows very well not to mow off the patches of poke salat we have growing wild.

# Thread: Blackout shades for the bedroom?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blackout-shades-for-the-bedroom.10531/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hemming — 2014-04-13T21:34:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blackout-shades-for-the-bedroom.10531/post-121342>

I've been thinking about getting some new blackout shades for my bedroom which would quite effectively block out all the light from the surrounding buildings. But how will that affect my natural circadian rhythm where I wake up when it gets light outside? I'm just afraid that I'll block out so much light that I wont sense the sun rising as I do now.

Any thoughts on this issue?

### kovita — 2014-04-13T21:50:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blackout-shades-for-the-bedroom.10531/post-121344>

we are after them as well. I can give you just a small token from my experience using a sleeping mask. I so wake up normally now around 6:30-7 am, when I keep the mask thorough the whole night, I would sleep at least 1 hour more. So definitely it is changing my sense of normal light cycle. I have as well experience from visits of my familiars in Spain. In Spain, blackout shades are a norm (at least in the area wher my familiars live). I never liked it, because I immediately (and our kids as well) change the circadian rhytm to the local one (wake up very late, go to bed very late, all meals shifted by 2-3 hours). So it is a tough call, because light from buildings around are bothering my night, but the same time the blackout shades would not roll out when the sunrise comes.You could use some electronics with timer to roll them out, but electronics...

### Hemming — 2014-04-13T21:57:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blackout-shades-for-the-bedroom.10531/post-121345>

Thanks for sharing your experiences, that is exactly what I'm concerned about too. I want no light at night but in the morning and I would prefer to keep at least my bedroom free of all electronics.

On a side note, I also hate the Spanish circadian rhythm

### nonchalant — 2014-04-15T19:05:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blackout-shades-for-the-bedroom.10531/post-121683>

I have blackout shades on my bedroom windows, but I keep a door ajar that gets some morning sunlight from another room. So it is pretty dark at night, but enought light in the morning to signal the sunrise.

### Hemming — 2014-04-15T22:03:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blackout-shades-for-the-bedroom.10531/post-121712>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=121683)
>
> I have blackout shades on my bedroom windows, but I keep a door ajar that gets some morning sunlight from another room. So it is pretty dark at night, but enought light in the morning to signal the sunrise.

Good workaround the issue  Unfortunately I can't do that in my apartment.

### cinnamon — 2014-04-16T03:24:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blackout-shades-for-the-bedroom.10531/post-121765>

> [Hemming said:](/goto/post?id=121712)
>
> Good workaround the issue  Unfortunately I can't do that in my apartment.

I am very sensitive to light at night these days. If I am exposed to much that is bright, I get hunger pangs within a few minutes. I suppose that's a clue that I've successfully trained my body to think it is getting fed promptly come morning? LOL

I put up blackout curtains, but _I turn back one little corner of the curtain_ so there is a crack of light allowed in. I have also taken up wearing a sleep mask (though that is usually abandoned sometime in the middle of the night). The quality of daylight coming through that crack once dawn has arrived is vastly different from the artificial street lighting during the night and seems to work well to let me know it's time to get up.

### Marie — 2014-04-16T05:02:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blackout-shades-for-the-bedroom.10531/post-121774>

I use blackout curtains, blinds, and a sleep mask.
Since I started sleeping on a magnetico, I wake at dawn, whether I want to ... or not.

If you are going to start investing in yourself, I'd buy a sleep mask and a magnetico.

### Hemming — 2014-04-16T13:58:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blackout-shades-for-the-bedroom.10531/post-121813>

Thanks for the replies. I think I'll go for the blinds after all. They will be set up in a way that will enable some light to enter the room.

### Hope — 2014-04-19T02:44:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blackout-shades-for-the-bedroom.10531/post-122462>
