### Nick — 2014-04-23T08:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123141>

Hi everyone,

I have been reading this thread with interest as this sort of activity cuts across my day day job and I may have some ideas to help sorting through this challenge. There are so many great ideas being suggested but the bit that is missing is the context to tie it all together.

I think we need to agree on the big picture about what this site is meant to achieve and then focus on the problems that need to be solved to achieve this.

To my way of thinking Jacks site is aimed to:

Change the way medicine is conducted clinically, starting with creating starfish that can spread the word.

If this is the reason Jack has created the site then this will be the northern star that we can all navigate the various problems that need to be solved by.

These are a list of problems that need to be solved in this context. I have quite a few ideas about answers to these and many have already been suggested but for the moment I would just like to tightly define the problems that need to be solved.

1\. The site needs to cater to wide variety of starfish, from the Neil's and Robbs's of the world down to brain fogged non technical family members. Both ends of the spectrum contain valuable starfish but have different requirements to learn what they require.

2\. There is no simple area for beginners to understand the big picture and get involved. Caroline spends ages talking to newbies trying to get them started, where a simple starting area would save a lot of time. This is worthy of a lot of discussion regarding what tools need to be built or reshaped.

3\. There is no clear area for professional to get the information they need to be armed to take on the system.

4\. Blogs - At the beginning of a series there is no real intro to show where the series will go and what are the critical areas to keep a eye on are.

5\. Forums - they are too diffuse and scattered. How do we keep all the critical info accessible and easy to find? Sitting ringside to Neil and randy being challenged by jack is priceless but how do we find these gems easily and quickly when we check the forum each morning?

6\. Forums - how do we go back and find great info several months later. Jack has dropped bomb after bomb in threads over the years but I have no idea how to find them now!

7\. Member - need to have quick access to all links easily for q&a's webinars etc

8\. N=1 for newbies, understanding this context is tricky and how to manage around it. This ties into what labs are important when not understanding the implications of the n =1.

9\. Links - how do we find links in forums, Facebook and webinars easily. There is a great video that I started watching from a blog link to the royal society but I don't know where to find it easily any more without first finding and opening a bunch of blogs.

10\. Keeping the info from fb and the site co-ordinated so that one place has all of the links.

11\. Case studies and success stories - these are hard to find but worth their weight in gold, the stories of tumors disappearing, mr pinkies families, the Aussie gymnast are great ways to help relate the hard science to great health outcomes.

12\. The prescriptions need to be in one place that is easy to find. Maybe as the years go by JK could update more of the context around the rx's to keep it in sync with the latest blogs

13\. It is hard to keep up with the terminology some days.

This is a quick list of the obvious problems that I can think of that would be nice to be addressed. Some are real quick fixes, other will need a lot of thought and planning.

The challenge here is to stick to basics without designing a gold plated Rolls Royce that jack can never build. We also need to keep a sense of perspective, the site is already amazing, it has changed people's lives forever and is full of great members and information. Jack has done a stunning job to get it to this point.

I hope this helps as a discussion point. I do this all the time at work and it is amazing how hard it is to keep a bunch of really smart people focused on solving specific problems with context rather than just coming up with disconnected great ideas.

Feel free to throw eggs at me if I'm not being helpfull!!!

Cheers

Nick

Sent from my iPad

### yewwei.tan — 2014-04-23T11:48:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123155>

As Jack as said many times, in order for the impact of the website to scale, we need to rely on community members stepping up to help educate those around them.

I personally echo Neil's want for an accelerated track for Healthcare Practitioners (his post -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...p-to-help-you-better.10616/page-3#post-123045](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/page-3#post-123045)) Ideally, these people should have the entire quilt laid out to them in whatever way possible. Comprehensibility and coherence isn't important here -- completeness of information is. I bet you can trust a Josh or a Neil to actively seek out the correct information and make the correct links.

\---
**Personal Gripe**

As a software developer that benefits hugely from Open Source Software, I personally do not subscribe to the philosophy of holding back information.

At least in software, we've discovered over time that you can give your product away completely for free. Most people use the product for free, but there's going to be some group of users, usually large corporations, who can't afford screw ups in their software, and have to integrate your new software offering into their own complex code base -- so you end up charging large companies lots of money for support fees to build a solution just for them. All this while, the little guy starting a software startup like me can still benefit from using really robust free software (and maybe end up becoming a large corp that ends up paying for software support). This is the business model behind many huge companies (RedHat with RHEL, Basho with Riak, etc ...)

Similarly, the Quilt forms the basic principles from which we derive our knowledge from. In my opinion all those principles should be laid out clearly in bullet form, focusing on the mechanism of action as much as possible, and not put much (if any) emphasis on case studies and diseases.

Here's an example of the style. I'm referencing the most recent podcast with Ameer Rosic, from which I made some notes of the first 5+ minutes -- [http://www.ameerrosic.com/how-to-biohack-your-biochemistry-is-the-redox-potential-the-missing-link/](http://www.ameerrosic.com/how-to-biohack-your-biochemistry-is-the-redox-potential-the-missing-link/):

Question: What is the redox potential?

1.  It's the electric charge on any membrane in your body, but the one that we're interested in is the one on the inner mitochondrial membrane
2.  The higher the charge, the lower the pH gradient needed to move Protons through the mitochondrial membrane.
3.  The mitochondria work on positively and negatively charged sub-atomic particles. Electrons (-) from food, and Protons (+) from water
4.  Mitochondria make huge amounts of protons, and within each proton is potential energy (H3O+)
5.  Both insufficient and excess proton flow can diminish your redox potential.
6.  When mitochondria produce protons, those protons need to be accepted by Water (to form H3O+).
7.  If these protons are not accepted by Water, they go on to form reactive oxygen species (ROS)
8.  These excess protons also lower pH in the cytosol. A low pH causes LESS electric charge over the inner mitochondrial membrane, and thus REDUCES your redox potential
9.  Repeat: excess protons decrease the electric charge on your inner mitochondrial membrane.
10.  A low electron flow can also diminish proton flow across the mitochondrial membrane

Question: How do you measure your redox potential?

-   The easiest way to measure the redox potential is the BUN/Creatinine ratio. It should be below 10:1, and ideally 7:1
-   The BUN/Creatinine ratio works because it is a measure of cellular hydration. Having water surrounding the mitochondria is KEY as described in point (6) and (7) in the previous question.

Question: What kills your redox potential?

-   Dehydration
-   EMF (calcium efflux)

I found that to be a hugely helpful starting point to explore the concept of the Redox Potential. It's almost a from-first-principles vantage point, from which the inquisitive mind will come away with more questions than answers. This forms the basis for the NeilBB's among us to do research and make connections.

For those who just want actionable steps, you've laid those out very well already in the Redox Rx blog post -- [http://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](http://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/)

Where you need to work your magic is in relating those principles to Hashi's, to Melasma, to Brain Cancer, to Hemachromatosis, to Lyme Disease, etc ... that in my mind is the unique value proposition that only someone with an intimate grasp of all the concepts in the Quilt can bring to the table.

\----
The ideal realistic scenario would be to have some sort of Wiki software, and then permit certain members of the community to contribute to collective Wikis that outline all the principles of the Quilt in detail.

Jack will have to seed all the necessary information by writing it out somewhere somehow. This doesn't have to be in a coherent form so long as the information is complete. From there, we can then rely on the community to do the grunt work of making sense of the information. We'll re-arrange, re-format, and make new connections to form a coherent piece, and Jack should act as a curator who stops us when we're running astray of the Quilt Principles.

In other words, my ideal Jackkruse.com would be:

-   A repository of ALL the principles of the Quilt
-   A central set of blog posts that act as "Gateways" into the Quilt Principles -- they show us how the Quilt applies in real-life examples
-   Jack acting as a curator of community generated content

\---
**Transcriptions of Webinars and Audio
**
Ameer has the entire interview with Jack in textual form in the link I showed above. Ben Greenfield also gets all his podcasts transcribed.

This is relatively cheap, but may or may not be helpful. Personally, I would have found a transcription of the Mitochondrial Rx Webinar very helpful, since that's the sort of info that is better retained when read as opposed listened to.

Sidenote: the Quantum Sleep blog seemed to be almost a word-for-word rendition of the webinar. Do you write up your webinars before delivering them? If so, then would it be possible to make those notes or text available on the same page that we view webinars on?​

\---

Apologies if the above sounds confrontational, and thanks for all the information that you've already put out there .

### ssj3 — 2014-04-23T11:49:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123156>

Dr Kruse,

I assume that the main goal of creating an online presence was to educate others? If so, then this is where the majority of your time should be focused and how content should be delivered.

I believe that most paying members have joined to not only solve personal issues but to gradually build an understanding of your methodology and how to apply your knowledge. Granted that the desired level of knowledge will vary amongst members, I see a lack of the basic building blocks as a cause for "poor" questions, your frustration in answering the same type of questions and member confusion in comprehending new information and using it to build upon existing information.

A possible solution would be to build a "course" where each week/month, you outline and provide resources about what members should understand. I would see this starting from the very basic physics/biology principles and building up overtime (you may need two streams of this to cater for already knowledgeable members). Blue sky would be to actually have the facility to confirm their understanding before progressing to the next stage (sorry, engineering background kicking in).

The benefit in gradually developing this over time is that you will not have to find all the time up-front, only small amounts whenever it becomes available. If the cost is of concern, you could have members sign-up for this in say a 6 month or yearly time frame.

I have actually been thinking about this for sometime, as I have been trying to go back through old blogs and posts to better my understanding, but I find it inefficient without a system in place.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-04-23T13:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123162>

I was thinking that an online trivia that could be played on the site would be cool to play with the concepts. There could be also a ranking so we can list the members top scores or something like that, I'm thinking of gamification of the quilt! It might seem a weird idea but, playing is a great way to learn! I'm thinking about something like this (inspired in brain/gut 6):

> Only one is correct. You should eat fruit when...
>
> a) Is seasonal in your geographic area
> b) On winter
> c) 5 pieces a day all-year round
> d) You have cravings for sweet things

There could be an explanation about why the answer is correct or not once the player answers. Maybe there could be different levels to play: the gut series, the CT series, quantum physics... or based on difficulty level, ie easy to advanced. They could also be centered on a topic so they may be linked at the end of a blog post to see if people understood it correctly! More question cards could be added as the quilt and the blogs develop in time. The possibilities are unlimited come to think about it. I think I might add it to my blog somewhere in the future! It would be fun to have such a game, wouldn't it?

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-23T14:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123163>

> [patgrif@hotmail.com said:](/goto/post?id=123100)
>
> Caroline: I know what you mean about the meanings of words or acynonmys. Like for quite a while I thought QED might stand for "Quad Erat Demonstandum" ... I was pretty sure it was NOT that but only recently I realize it stands for "Quantum Electro Dynamics" which now make all the sense in the world.

If Jack were to give the people who administer the blog software a list of his abbreviations, they should be able to arrange it so that whenever he uses one of them the text is automatically adjusted on posting so that what it stands for will show on mouse-over. You'll see this sometimes on blogs -- there'll be a little dotted line under the letters to clue you in that you can see more on mouse-over.

Effectively, a snippet of code could, for example, change every QED to:

Code:

```
<abbr title="Quantum Electro Dynamics">QED</abbr>
```

(You don't as a reader see what's in the angle-brackets, but the "title" will come up when you put the mouse-cursor on the word.)

Nick…super overview and suggestions! It is getting to the principals that can organize the material and letting people in at many different levels of comprehension that is the tricky bit…

Transitioning the material from "how" it was written in serial format (next week, next blog, next month) to focussing on "why" it was written (the principals that you mention in your post) is necessary. People can't take three years of their lives to reproduce the process the way those of us who have been here for awhile have done.

The people who work with you are really lucky!

### Andrea — 2014-04-23T15:12:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123172>

I seem to be the only one to quite like things the way they are. Except for a missing date index.

At first, I did find the site tricky and overwhelming (and crazy), but... One thing that attracts me to this place is that \*I\* need to do a lot thinking. In a way, Dr. K. has served us loads of information on a silver platter, he just doesn't force feed us.
I love to see dr. K.'s thought processes and, in an odd way, I kind of enjoy my frustration from not understanding...
I can't even pretend to understand 1 ppm of all the stuff written in the blogs or on this forum, but it is exactly what got me hooked. If there were simple and clear instructions and explanations, or flashy illustrations etc., I'd probably run away.

I'm very grateful for the work dr. K. has done and is sharing with us. And I like that he's doing it in his own way. The contents of this site are different from other people's blogs, so why shouldn't the way things are presented be different as well?

My top 3 wants:
\- A date index (for the blogs and podcasts).
\- Post all the good and important stuff on the blog or possibly in the forum, rather than Facebook/Twitter/etc.
\- Fix the member's dashboard issues that others have written about; after all, these are the people who have paid $$$.

### BJK77 — 2014-04-23T15:40:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123177>

Something I always thought would be interesting and helpful is a "Day in the Life of" feature . . . maybe starting with Jack and then showcasing other members. Kind of a way to explain how they manage all the protocols and what they are finding that works for them. I'm not talking a blog post where they detail their history, etc, etc. Just a quick bullet point example of what a day may be like for them: what they're eating, how/when they do CT, how they get in earthing throughout the day, what they do to follow the light cycle (ie get sun 1st thing in AM, put blue blockers on at dusk).

Of course it should always be emphasized that everything is n=1, but I think that would be very helpful to newbies to show them that even though this all seems so overwhelming initially it is practical and can be done. It would also be good for oldies to review at times just to keep us on top of things. It's so easy to forget little pieces here and there when we're learning so much and making so many adjustments to our lifestyles.

Well I will be talking about the future in the May Q & A......so be there if you want to hear it live. May 4th.

### caroline — 2014-04-23T22:16:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123234>

> [Rerun said:](/goto/post?id=123172)
>
> I seem to be the only one to quite like things the way they are. Except for a missing date index.
>
> At first, I did find the site tricky and overwhelming (and crazy), but... One thing that attracts me to this place is that \*I\* need to do a lot thinking. In a way, Dr. K. has served us loads of information on a silver platter, he just doesn't force feed us.
> I love to see dr. K.'s thought processes and, in an odd way, I kind of enjoy my frustration from not understanding...
> I can't even pretend to understand 1 ppm of all the stuff written in the blogs or on this forum, but it is exactly what got me hooked. If there were simple and clear instructions and explanations, or flashy illustrations etc., I'd probably run away.
>
> I'm very grateful for the work dr. K. has done and is sharing with us. And I like that he's doing it in his own way. The contents of this site are different from other people's blogs, so why shouldn't the way things are presented be different as well?
>
> My top 3 wants:
> \- A date index (for the blogs and podcasts).
> \- Post all the good and important stuff on the blog or possibly in the forum, rather than Facebook/Twitter/etc.
> \- Fix the member's dashboard issues that others have written about; after all, these are the people who have paid $$$.

I love everything you say here. From the beginning my understanding was that dr. K. Is teaching us to think - he always said he wouldn't dumb it down - if we have to read it 20 times - so be it.

It can be so hard and frustrating but I am learning amazing things and I wouldn't trade this life altering experience for anything.

I think it was hard for all of us getting started - just as it is now for newbies.....but that has been part of the experience - part of our own personal learning curve. Some people take a look and then can't be bothered - so unless it is spoon fed to them they really aren't interested .... In my opinion, that isn't what this is all about.

I have spent a lot of time with newbies - but I love it ....and it challenges me to make sure I know what I am talking about!

There are always going to be people that can't really be bothered anyway .....

There are lots of people that only come here when they want to sort out a problem and don't get involved in the community or give back and read others journals and offer help.

The forum has been great for developing relationships and we all try to help each other and also help the new people. I think it is amazing when one of us has an aha moment and then shares it with the rest.

Neil and Rob and Josh are great at lending a hand to us scientifically challenged!

Except for technical things ...I personally think that Dr. Kruse has done a great job.

Caroline spends ages talking to newbies trying to get them started, where a simple starting area would save a lot of time. This is worthy of a lot of discussion regarding what tools need to be built or reshaped.

> Sent from my iPad

guys, i just posted this in the cave but should have put it here.

ive been trying to organise a visual flowchart/diagram to easily tie all concepts from the blog into one visual diagram/flowchart for ease of understanding for newcomers, after reading the comments here in the help thread.

does anyone want to help/throw ideas around in this thread for a format?

i am thinking the three legged stool of water light and magnetism in the center, with arrows and lines between them indicating how they tie together

we could perhaps then have various threads emerging outward as things get more detailed...

anyways any help would be welcomed, ive been over a few ideas in my head and want to start working on it to help the community and get newbs to have a decent understanding on firts sight, which will save Jack the frustration of harping over the same stuff again and again, as this tends to slow the momentum inherent in this type of energetic learning.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-04-24T02:18:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123249>

Josh, I started a similar idea some time ago, it's my own version of the quilt, which I'd like to keep expanding as I learn more and more, not just from this site but from all places, that includes, of course, my ongoing degree on nutrition. It's in catalan but you get the idea:
[http://test.corredors.cat/albert83/own/graph.html](http://test.corredors.cat/albert83/own/graph.html)

It uses a simple HTML with the Javascript InfoVis toolkit with an XML file to load the data. It pretty basic, the library allows for quite more magic but this would be a starting point.

I didn't took it to the next level for the moment because I'm not quite satisfied with the result, the problem being a software developer is that I realize it could always be done in a better way, I want a database not a static file, a way to enter the data more easily and also to query it.... so for now I keep gathering ideas and wait for a perfect timing. I like the graph idea because it allows to tie a lot of things altogether, I'm planing to move into the D3 library but it needs time to learn how it works and right now I'm lazy for it. I also think that if I could manage to do a 3D version it could get still better because I'd add "layers" that mimic the different biology levels, from the universe to the quantum level, etc... maybe I'd better just get started but I know first hand that If I deploy a lot of data in a certain way it'll probably stay like this forever because right now I cannot afford the time to invest tons of hours implementing the perfect idea. But maybe I do on holidays, I'm quite a random person so you never know...

I have also been mulling over the thread comments and one of my Father's oft repeated sayings comes to mind..

> “You can please some of the people all of the time, you can please all of the people some of the time, but you can’t please all of the people all of the time”.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-24T05:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123271>

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=123155)
>
> As Jack as said many times, in order for the impact of the website to scale, we need to rely on community members stepping up to help educate those around them.
>
> I personally echo Neil's want for an accelerated track for Healthcare Practitioners (his post -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...p-to-help-you-better.10616/page-3#post-123045](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/page-3#post-123045)) Ideally, these people should have the entire quilt laid out to them in whatever way possible. Comprehensibility and coherence isn't important here -- completeness of information is. I bet you can trust a Josh or a Neil to actively seek out the correct information and make the correct links.
>
> \---
> **Personal Gripe**
>
> As a software developer that benefits hugely from Open Source Software, I personally do not subscribe to the philosophy of holding back information.
>
> At least in software, we've discovered over time that you can give your product away completely for free. Most people use the product for free, but there's going to be some group of users, usually large corporations, who can't afford screw ups in their software, and have to integrate your new software offering into their own complex code base -- so you end up charging large companies lots of money for support fees to build a solution just for them. All this while, the little guy starting a software startup like me can still benefit from using really robust free software (and maybe end up becoming a large corp that ends up paying for software support). This is the business model behind many huge companies (RedHat with RHEL, Basho with Riak, etc ...)
>
> Similarly, the Quilt forms the basic principles from which we derive our knowledge from. In my opinion all those principles should be laid out clearly in bullet form, focusing on the mechanism of action as much as possible, and not put much (if any) emphasis on case studies and diseases.
>
> Here's an example of the style. I'm referencing the most recent podcast with Ameer Rosic, from which I made some notes of the first 5+ minutes -- [http://www.ameerrosic.com/how-to-biohack-your-biochemistry-is-the-redox-potential-the-missing-link/](http://www.ameerrosic.com/how-to-biohack-your-biochemistry-is-the-redox-potential-the-missing-link/):
>
>
> Question: What is the redox potential?
>
> 1.  It's the electric charge on any membrane in your body, but the one that we're interested in is the one on the inner mitochondrial membrane
> 2.  The higher the charge, the lower the pH gradient needed to move Protons through the mitochondrial membrane.
> 3.  The mitochondria work on positively and negatively charged sub-atomic particles. Electrons (-) from food, and Protons (+) from water
> 4.  Mitochondria make huge amounts of protons, and within each proton is potential energy (H3O+)
> 5.  Both insufficient and excess proton flow can diminish your redox potential.
> 6.  When mitochondria produce protons, those protons need to be accepted by Water (to form H3O+).
> 7.  If these protons are not accepted by Water, they go on to form reactive oxygen species (ROS)
> 8.  These excess protons also lower pH in the cytosol. A low pH causes LESS electric charge over the inner mitochondrial membrane, and thus REDUCES your redox potential
> 9.  Repeat: excess protons decrease the electric charge on your inner mitochondrial membrane.
> 10.  A low electron flow can also diminish proton flow across the mitochondrial membrane
>
>
> Question: How do you measure your redox potential?
>
> -   The easiest way to measure the redox potential is the BUN/Creatinine ratio. It should be below 10:1, and ideally 7:1
> -   The BUN/Creatinine ratio works because it is a measure of cellular hydration. Having water surrounding the mitochondria is KEY as described in point (6) and (7) in the previous question.
>
> Question: What kills your redox potential?
>
> -   Dehydration
> -   EMF (calcium efflux)
>
> I found that to be a hugely helpful starting point to explore the concept of the Redox Potential. It's almost a from-first-principles vantage point, from which the inquisitive mind will come away with more questions than answers. This forms the basis for the NeilBB's among us to do research and make connections.
>
> For those who just want actionable steps, you've laid those out very well already in the Redox Rx blog post -- [http://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](http://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/)
>
> Where you need to work your magic is in relating those principles to Hashi's, to Melasma, to Brain Cancer, to Hemachromatosis, to Lyme Disease, etc ... that in my mind is the unique value proposition that only someone with an intimate grasp of all the concepts in the Quilt can bring to the table.
>
> \----
> The ideal realistic scenario would be to have some sort of Wiki software, and then permit certain members of the community to contribute to collective Wikis that outline all the principles of the Quilt in detail.
>
> Jack will have to seed all the necessary information by writing it out somewhere somehow. This doesn't have to be in a coherent form so long as the information is complete. From there, we can then rely on the community to do the grunt work of making sense of the information. We'll re-arrange, re-format, and make new connections to form a coherent piece, and Jack should act as a curator who stops us when we're running astray of the Quilt Principles.
>
> In other words, my ideal Jackkruse.com would be:
>
> -   A repository of ALL the principles of the Quilt
> -   A central set of blog posts that act as "Gateways" into the Quilt Principles -- they show us how the Quilt applies in real-life examples
> -   Jack acting as a curator of community generated content
>
>
> \---
> **Transcriptions of Webinars and Audio
> **
> Ameer has the entire interview with Jack in textual form in the link I showed above. Ben Greenfield also gets all his podcasts transcribed.
>
> This is relatively cheap, but may or may not be helpful. Personally, I would have found a transcription of the Mitochondrial Rx Webinar very helpful, since that's the sort of info that is better retained when read as opposed listened to.
>
>
> Sidenote: the Quantum Sleep blog seemed to be almost a word-for-word rendition of the webinar. Do you write up your webinars before delivering them? If so, then would it be possible to make those notes or text available on the same page that we view webinars on?​
>
>
> \---
>
> Apologies if the above sounds confrontational, and thanks for all the information that you've already put out there .

Well make it a community project............

### Nick — 2014-04-28T09:41:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123622>

An area for Newbies

I think we need an area of the site where we can point people who know nothing about JK. There needs to be an easy way to bring a potential starfish into the fold and give them the basic tools, understanding and confidence to take charge of their own health. From here they can move onto the complex blogs when they have these basics squared away in their head. This serves the

This could include

1\. A big picture easy to understand blog that describes where JK is has gone and is going

2\. Links to the most important early blogs to get you started e.g. Hormones 101, Leptin

3\. A very high level description to cover the blogs from BG onwards covering EMF, Water, EZ and QED with links to key blogs

4\. Links to all of the Rx's

5\. Basic labs tied to context to get people started

6\. Some success stories

### Nick — 2014-04-28T09:43:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123623>

### Nick — 2014-04-28T09:49:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123626>

An easier way to start a blog series

The blogs are gold! I would love it though if at the beginning of a new series the first blog could be devoted to a big picture of where the series will go, what elements are critical to keep an eye on. I think that this could be broken down into a description of the important elements for newbies and a more complex version for professionals. This way we can put meat on the bones as we read the series but know what the skeleton is going to look like before we start and as we make the journey through a new series.

### Nick — 2014-04-28T09:54:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123627>

Forums - Boom moments

I think we need some dedicated threads that can capture 'Boom' moments from Jack and archive them in one place. For instance if JK drops a series of bombs on a thread, can the moderators copy and paste that with a link to the original thread into a separate dedicated forum. These comments are often critically important and we don't want to stop Jack dropping into a thread and doing his thing, but to have an archive of all of the important comments would be terrific.

### Nick — 2014-04-28T10:04:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123628>

A new "What is" forum

Personally I would love a new section of the site where anyone can help describe a part of the body or a part of science. This should be community based and could really help leverage the knowledge of many of the members to help newbies and everyone else. I was thinking that it would be neat to have one thread per element that can build over months and years as the blogs grow out. I often ask myself basic questions like "what is an amino acid", "what is DHEA", "What is the PPP", "What is mTor" etc etc.

Googling these looses the JK context which is critical. I think that this could build over time and any member can start a new thread. These should be listed in alphabetical order and be a community built resource to help everyone understand the blogs and webinars better. The connections between all of the parts is often the most complex to understand and anything we can do help understand this should potentially be very powerful.

### Nick — 2014-04-28T10:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-123629>

BTW Jack, I think you should rush slowly when it comes to rebuilding your site and work on what is the biggest problem first. I suspect it is getting newbies armed up and understanding the basics so that they can then tell other people about what they have learnt.

There is so much that is great about the site and although it might be creaking and groaning in places it is a mighty resource that works well. Thank you again!

### tellmisty — 2014-05-03T20:58:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-124910>

A few of pointers to help:

> [Nick said:](/goto/post?id=123622)
>
> An area for Newbies
>
> I think we need an area of the site where we can point people who know nothing about JK. There needs to be an easy way to bring a potential starfish into the fold and give them the basic tools, understanding and confidence to take charge of their own health. From here they can move onto the complex blogs when they have these basics squared away in their head. This serves the
>
> This could include
>
> 1\. A big picture easy to understand blog that describes where JK is has gone and is going
>
> 2\. Links to the most important early blogs to get you started e.g. Hormones 101, Leptin
>
> 3\. A very high level description to cover the blogs from BG onwards covering EMF, Water, EZ and QED with links to key blogs
>
> 4\. Links to all of the Rx's
>
> 5\. Basic labs tied to context to get people started
>
> 6\. Some success stories

\* If someone is new, they should click on "New to Jackkruse.com?" on the home page. Lots of getting started stuff there, including a video from Dr. Kruse advising people on how to get started. There is also a "Getting Started Guide" in the main nav to help newbies start to find their way around, including an overview of many of the protocols. When they are ready for Biohacking, they should subscribe to "Biohacking 101." We give a good rundown of labs, etc.

There is no way to make this stuff 8th grade level ... people are going to have to roll up their sleeves if they want to benefit from the content on the site. The weak need not apply. ;-)

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=122895)
>
> I love the forum. I also love the webinars. Things that will help:
>
> Single log-in. I shouldn't need 4 different log ins to access different content on the site
>
> If I've purchased a content EMF Webinar, Heal Your Hormones bootcamp - I should be able to access that in the same way.
>
> For example When I log in to my "member dashboard" I should be able to see all the programs I've purchased and then be able to access them at will.
>
> So I should be able to see all the EMF Bootcamp Webinars/Q&A and Heal Your Hormones Bootcamp/Q&A on the same page as my Membership content - Silver/Gold/Platinum. If I can't see or access what I've purchased and I have to email support every time I want to watch something - total PITA.
> I'm still having problems accessing those two bootcamp, yet I'm supposed to have access to content.
>
> Also I shouldn't have to go check back emails to see if I missed the webinar info.

\* All member info is in the member dashboard, in one place ... UNLESS you signed up for the Optimal Reset. That had to be set up under a separate login. Your bootcamps will be listed in your member dashboard IF you used the same email address. If you don't see it, [email support for help](mailto:support@jackkruse.com). If you signed up for the Optimal Reset with the same email address, however, when you login to your dashboard, you will see a login link to access the Optimal Reset.

\* If you are a paid member, you do not need to "wait" for an email to access the webinar. The webinars are all loaded into your member dashboard, so just login. You will see it. You may also access the webinars under "notes" in the secret facebook groups for each member level. This functions the exact same way every month. If you don't remember your password, click "lost your password" and check your email to reset. If you have further issues or want to be added to the secret facebook group for your member level, email [support@jackkruse.com](mailto:support@jackkruse.com).

\* The much-requested blog index exists. [The blog is indexed by subject here](http://jackkruse.com/blog-index/). [It's indexed by series (which is essentially date order) here](http://jackkruse.com/table-of-contents/).

I think part of the challenge some are having is that they have not explored all the things that were done over the last 6 months or so to update the functionality of the site. Many things people are asking for in this thread, in fact, exist on the site now; you've just have to leave the beaten path of the blog and forums. There is a simple navigation and key things people look for / ask about in the right sidebar. Start clickin'!

Hope this helps.

Dr Kruse, I am 73 years old and you and your blog are the most exciting thing that I have experienced in my 6 years of retirement. I could see how stretched you were and was worried about you continuing this load. First, I would hope that the blog posts and associated Q&As would maintain the same links, at least for a few months as I have copied all blog posts into Word documents and am now working on copying the Q&As. I use these off line to read as it is difficult for me to read on a computer. Secondly, I have 3 different accounts and passwords on your website. I would like to see one account access all that I have rights to. Thirdly, I would like to see an index of the blog posts, listed by the date they were published.

I feel like I am doing the most important thing I can do for my family and grandkids by investing so much effort into becoming Optimal. I have a long history of poor health, which is improving, and I want to share your knowledge with those that I am close to. You are a hero to me. I could go on to say many positive things about what you have done for me but I can sum this up by saying your blog is a God Send for me in my retirement years.

After reading the comments in this section, I had one more comment. I focus mostly on the blog posts and their Q&As. If you post something on the forum that is not covered on your blog posts, an index to these posts that you deem important would be very helpful to me. I am most interested in your core ideals and posts regardless of where they are on the blog. Also editing errors in posts do not bother me. Your knowledge is pure Gold and I do not mind separating the gravel from the Gold. What ever works for you works for me, just keep going.

And yes, I do love you.

### yewwei.tan — 2014-05-14T01:31:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-126776>

> [Gracelind said:](/goto/post?id=126765)
>
> Absolutely! The journals are valuable for all members to read. They serve both informational and social purposes. Some really insightful gems get dropped in journals.
>
> What I suggest is to bring the journals inside one layer of security so Google doesn't pick them up. I'm a member of several forums where some areas are completely public while other areas can be easily accessed by administrators and registered members but not the data miners.
>
> It's disconcerting to search Google and find my personal medical discussion pop up.

This is easily done by adding "noindex" and "nofollow" meta tags to all webpages in the Optimal Journal section, which should be a one-line configuration change on most forum platforms. I have no experience with this platform, but I'm sure Jack's team can easily sort that out.

If you want to be even more sure, then just change the subdomain from "forum" to "forums" and attach a generic 301 or 302 redirect rule to all forum.jackkruse.com/\* paths, which when combined with the previous step, should invalidate the search indexes of a well-behaved search engine (no guarantees that search engines are "well-behaved" though). Again, I'm very sure this is an easy few-line change for the devs behind the site.

Of course, that only protects you from search engines -- real humans can still just click links and find your journal. If you want added protection, then you need to get the devs to implement some sort of access-control scheme, where only people with Bronze, Silver, and Gold status can access selected sections of the forum. I don't know how easy this is to configure on this forum platform. (And of course, there's nothing stopping a member of the site from compromising your information if they choose to be malicious).

Tech Doomsday Sidenote: a rule of thumb is to assume that anything you put on the internet is compromised. That includes all emails not sent over PGP or equivalent combined auth-enc mechanisms, all SMSes, etc ....

### kyrakitty — 2014-06-04T20:22:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-129665>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=129572)
>
> Somebody makes a study guide and I am all for it.

If I knew the date order you released all your material along with the titles of each piece you released I would be happy to make a PDF study guide (it will probably actually be several PDF's as there is so much info for one PDF).

Here is how I would do it:

1.  I would make a study guide with a table of contents and after the table of contents the material would be there.

2.  The table of contents would list all your blogs, webinars and the occasional relevant forum post in chronological order from oldest to most recent.

3.  I would specifically make each table of contents item start with the date the info was released FIRST...followed by the title of the blog, webinar or forum posting.

4.  Only a few select chosen forum threads would make the study guide list. Ones that bring new info onto the table that is not already covered in a blog or webinar.
5.  Regardless of when "The Quilt" was made I would list that one first

Here is an example of the chronological table of contents study guide would look like:

**STUDY GUIDE**
Following the bread crumbs in the order they were given
_\*\*\*Note most info is free to the public but in order to view the below mentioned webinars you must purchase a membership... sign up!_​

-   -   THE QUILT

    -   01/15/2010 Title of blog goes here

    -   01/20/2010 Title of webinar goes here
    -   01/25/210 Title of blog goes here

    -   01/27/2010 Title of webinar Q&A (to above webinar) goes here
    -   02/13/2010 Title of blog goes here
    -   02/15/2010 Title of blog goes here
    -   02/17/2010 Tile of forum post goes here
    -   03/01/2010 Tile of blog goes here
    -   and on it goes until I reach the current blog as shown below....
    -   06/01/2014 ORGANIZATIONAL STRUCTURAL FAILURE #4: HUMAN PRIONS

I am sure there are hundreds of better ways to make this study guide but this is the easiest, I think, as others can use this main one to create tailored study guides that cater to specific conditions, or topics by using this main one.

Another option is to make the above table of contents into a page on the website with hyperlinks that link to the individual pieces of work. BUT it would mean that the website would need to make sure the website page addresses do not change or the links go dead. It is probably less work, in the long haul, to stick all the info into a PDF and not have to deal with keeping up weblink hassles and creating trouble ticket after trouble ticket.... as you already know.

### ATL_Paleo — 2014-06-05T03:35:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-129717>

I went back and looked at my notes about the blog. The first blog was published on June 1, 2011. ..... So Happy Three Year Anniversary Dr. Kruse.

Couple of observations jumped out at me ......

1) Blogs were published much more often, but they were also much smaller in size. ... Might be an idea to return to smaller more bite-size blogs.

2) I am very fortunate to have found the blog at the very beginning.

3) Many of the questions being currently asked in the Forum have their answers in older blogs .... especially the first 6 months or so.

4) The blogs clearly build on the previous ones. Unless a new reader is a highly trained health professional, it is probably not a good idea to start with the current blog. .... In this case, traditional schooling methods have some merits. Grade school is the foundation for middle school, pre-med is the foundation for medical school, etc.

5) I believe people that don't go back and start with the first blogs are inadvertently setting themselves up for failure. .... The exception would be the topics listed in the Quick Start Guide and any of the Rx blogs.

### kyrakitty — 2014-06-05T04:15:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-129727>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=129705)
>
> Kyra I doubt people will help with this........they always complain about the difficulty of the blogs........Then they complain when I tell them to collect DHA to collect electrons and make it simple. You just can not make people happy. Change is two sides of the same coin........what determines the side we see is our redox potential. It is also what determines the choice we make in life.........good redox leads to optimal dopamine and great choices........and conversely the opposite happens. This site is filled with people with a mixed redox sink.........hence why no one decision will make anyone truly satisfied.

Yes, True.

It is difficult telling folks it is not possible for them to make good decisions because their brain doesn't have enough electrons. When I have told clients this they go blank in the eyes. Some feel insulted instead of looking at that statement as a fact, based on quantam, not a judgement and then doing something to improve their brain so they make better choices.

I have clients who ask me the exact same questions over and over and over. They simply cannot process the information I tell them that I learn here. It's like I am speaking Chinese. After a while instead of repeating myself I tell them. They don't have enough electrons in their brain to change what they are doing and then I explain below:

"Open up the hood of your car and look at the battery. The battery has a positive and negative terminal if you open up the chamber on top of the battery you will see water. Water connects to negative and positive. Same thing happens in your body. What connects water to every other part of your body? The protein called Collagen. It’s the wires that go off your terminals that go to your starter that start your car. What happens in the electronics in your house, what happens in your car, what happens everywhere around you also happens in you. This is the scale that we all need to understand.

When we drink water and then sit in the sun what does the sunlight do to water? It charge separates it into negative and positive charges. This is where the redox potential starts. And if this process is broken in you.. you become broken. You set the stage for heart disease, atherosclerosis, autoimmunity, neurodegeneration and cancer. What determines what disease you get? How low the environmental sink is of how deep your redox problem is. Your main goal is to protect your redox potential. **To find out exactly where it is at now, get it to it’s optimal level and then protect it.** "

I personally am half way there to finding out exactly where my redox is, via tons and tons of lab work and other tests which (results start coming back next week), next step will be to optimize it and then protect it. This study guide will help me.. and I really do need to start from the beginning and work forward to the most recent post. I've watched all the webinars but I need to to the reading of the blogs.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-06-06T00:52:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-129883>

Yep, but I doubt we would find any source that reunites such bang for the buck in terms of putting things like biology in simple terms that most can understand and being very accurate regarding with what we talk here, at the same time. That's why this site is unique in its own way. But, even like this, I find it helpful to know the basics about science from the lens of the rest of the world, so you can argue then when you know better . Some of the videos are quite up to date, the one that explains enthalpy and entropy I think is really useful because it's a critical concept that I find it's not easy to grasp, etc. Good starting point at least. Reading Jacks posts with no background must suck, I don't have much, but I cannot imagine how someone who has zero can get here to improve their health and get something useful from the blogs without having to do a big act of faith...

### Penny — 2014-06-06T02:38:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-129902>

I think you might be stretched a bit thin as you have a big heart - I mean think about all the places where people have access to you - and the idea was to get away from all this media... Do your blogs with whatever frequency you want - I like once a week - it's very exciting when a new one comes out - answer the questions related to the blog that require clarification - this is what makes the gold in the comments and skip giving free medical advice that has nothing to do with the message - I mean it's very nice of you, but you too need a life - the idea of this blog is to present information with a quantum view - not to solve every medical problem that comes along - very nice of you but save yourself...

I also like the forest before the trees approach to any writing, but really, what you are talking about is quite complicated to understand but simple enough to put into practice - you don't have to be a physicist to stick your face in some cold water and eat seasonally and walk in nature barefoot -

One last suggestion concerning your blog format - I read a blog once that was technical and the guy actually had laid out vocabulary links and all kinds of stuff so before you even got to his message, the groundwork was there - you might try something like that but really, I find your writing quite good and going back a rereading the stuff uncovers a pearl or two each time

The last gripe which would be easy to implement software-wise is the "by date" order of the blogs is now gone and there is this mass of blogs listed by disease... most people here had the advantage of reading these blogs in order so they made more sense as the info built upon the previous posts... now, if you try to access the blogs in the order they were posted, it is impossible - this would be very beneficial IMHO -

### Melanie Procter — 2014-06-21T03:49:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131625>

They say love is blind and this indicates I am in love with this website and the supportive group here. I see nothing wrong and just feel bliss that I was so lucky to find this blog, forum and all you good people. This feedback is probably useless. I only find perfection and true unconditional service here. I am not a new agey kind of person. I am just a lifelong researcher who has just wanted to really understand how things work. I found what I needed here in more abundance than I could ever imagine and with all you good people as support. I would like to say what is wrong, but as I said, love is blind.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-06-21T05:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131629>

> [Melanie Procter said:](/goto/post?id=131625)
>
> They say love is blind and this indicates I am in love with this website and the supportive group here. I see nothing wrong and just feel bliss that I was so lucky to find this blog, forum and all you good people. This feedback is probably useless. I only find perfection and true unconditional service here. I am not a new agey kind of person. I am just a lifelong researcher who has just wanted to really understand how things work. I found what I needed here in more abundance than I could ever imagine and with all you good people as support. I would like to say what is wrong, but as I said, love is blind.

Well, I am packing for the beach.........and my wife saw this and told me I needed to say something about it.........before we left. I need to disconnect this week in a big way. I have three podcasts scheduled on different topics and have a lot on my mind about a lot of things this Friday night.

So is love really blind or is it brave? Living is easy if your eyes are closed or are not capable of seeing. If love were blind it would usurp your mind. What we all think is love really is just another illusion. Instead, we should elevate our mind to meet the emotions of the day.

How brave are you this weekend? Do you step across the line for your life? Do you make life count by standing above the fray? Some of us understand the best things in life are never free. We are willing to pay the cost, because when we do pay that price we trade some of courage to become fulfilled. It takes bravery to leave the "familiar" to go after your dreams. When my back was against the wall and my faith under siege, life brought me to the point where I had to know that I was good enough to follow my convictions and my beliefs. Well, that was "the summer of my discontent". Pressure and stress reveals the true character of both a rock and a person by creating either a diamond or a handful of sand, on the one hand, and creating either a hero or a coward on the other. I stand here today abiding in a state where my intuition has brought the wow factor to every day of my life now. This weekend, tap your bravery and courage and do what you were born to do. Dare to be naive and share your voice with the world..........

### Melanie Procter — 2014-06-21T05:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131630>

Here! Here! We are all soooooooooooo grateful to Dr. Kruse and his wife, the hidden hero behind the cause. Thanks for the inspiration and encouragement to share our own voice! As you and your wife retreat this week, we'll go out and be your voice to the world. So, kick back and grab a few electrons!

But do tell us how we can listen to your podcasts live before you depart. We don't want to miss a thing, as we are your foot soldiers.

Have a great time you guys and forget about us and all of our questions. You've left enough material to keep us busy for the rest of our lives!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-06-21T15:02:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131649>

You have an obligation to yourself only. You can only change yourself. You can’t let other people tell you who you are. You have to decide that for yourself. Health improvements always begin with I, not we. Here is a foundational issue about wellness.........the truth never changes, but you must. You must get on the path of truth and that path is lined with all the hard quantum physics I am sharing with you.

The reality of wellness is daunting. The closer you get to wellness in your life the more friends you will lose. People gravitate to you when you are average because it makes them feel comfortable with you. You must seek to be uncomfortable because optimal wellness is not found on a path of average moderation.

See when you decide to pursue greatness within you it makes everyone including those who love you an uncomfortable feeling. Sometimes you need to be prepared for the loss of people................

If you think this is wrong just think about all the people who once came here who have long since left................and then consider why the left. Optimal wellness truth path makes all of us uncomfortable fundamentally. I have told many of you get used to your discomfort. It is the key metric in hitting your goals long term.

As a physician I have learned many good lessons from my patients. People go on making bad decisions and remaining ill because they just can't resist natural forces that act upon our cells. They would rather have a pill of that ill or something to treat a symptom that brings quick relief. Moreover, many of them may know perfectly well that they are possibly buying a month's pleasure with a life's discomfort. I tell them that upfront. I make them understand the choice they are making. I have found over my life that all discomfort comes from suppressing your true identity. When you are sick you create a new identity and reality and you fall into it. Think about David the golf pro who was on here a few months ago getting pissed off about autism. He believes it can not be reversed and I do and that fundamentally destroyed his beliefs and paradigms. He has not been heard from since. This is the inconvenient truth about wellness...........it is not just a physical battle. You have to do battle with yourself and your dogma to beat any disease.

**Discomfort brings engagement and change. Discomfort means you're doing something that others were unlikely to do, because they're hiding out in the comfortable zone. When your uncomfortable actions lead to success, your body finally begins to rewards you and your mind then brings you back for more of that Rx.**

### Jack Kruse — 2014-06-21T15:37:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131656>

I dont' try communicate with folks on their level, I try my best to connect with them on my level. Connection is empowerment. There is a very real difference between making "contact" and making a “connection” with someone else. Contact may lead to a one-time transaction, whereas, connection always leads to a life-time transformation. When you connect - which "investment" holds potential for the greater return with people?

Modern humans make contact thru social media. Reality is that humans contain invisible threads to nature and they bind us most strongly to our life. Today, technology allows us to contact many people together, but we are all dying of loneliness, in spite of these contacts. Reconnection is the key to survival. Disconnection is the key to destruction. The interesting thing - the same key opens both doors.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-06-21T15:40:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131657>

You must believe in your cause, because transformation is a one-way ticket. A caterpillar becomes a butterfly, and there is no turning back. When you stretch your canvas it never returns to the size it was. When you do this consistently, you begin to paint on an inspired plane. Transformation begins within. Stop living with your dysfunctions because they determine how you live and see life, Stop defending now and fight for what might be. Become invested in changing your status quo. Settling in life, is a slippery slope leading to mediocrity.

### Josh — 2014-06-21T20:02:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131666>

A SUFI OF THE ORDER OF THE NAQSHBANDIS WAS ASKED, ’YOUR ORDER’S NAME MEANS
LITERALLY ”THE DESIGNERS”. WHAT DO YOU DESIGN, AND WHAT USE IS IT?’
HE SAID, ’WE DO A GREAT DEAL OF DESIGNING, AND IT IS MOST USEFUL. HERE IS A
PARABLE OF ONE SUCH FORM:
’UNJUSTLY IMPRISONED, A TINSMITH WAS ALLOWED TO RECEIVE A RUG WOVEN BY HIS
WIFE. HE PROSTRATED HIMSELF UPON THE RUG DAY AFTER DAY TO SAY HIS PRAYERS,
AND AFTER SOME TIME HE SAID TO HIS JAILERS, ”I AM POOR AND WITHOUT HOPE, AND
YOU ARE WRETCHEDLY PAID. BUT I AM A TINSMITH. BRING ME TIN AND TOOLS AND I SHALL
MAKE SMALL ARTEFACTS WHICH YOU CAN SELL IN THE MARKET, AND WE WILL BOTH
BENEFIT.”
’THE GUARDS AGREED TO THIS, AND PRESENTLY THEY AND THE TINSMITH WERE BOTH
MAKING A PROFIT, FROM WHICH THEY BOUGHT FOOD AND COMFORTS FOR THEMSELVES.
’THEN, ONE DAY, WHEN THE GUARDS WENT TO THE CELL, THE DOOR WAS OPEN, AND HE
WAS GONE.
’MANY YEARS LATER, WHEN THIS MAN’S INNOCENCE HAD BEEN ESTABLISHED, THE MAN
WHO HAD IMPRISONED HIM ASKED HIM HOW HE HAD ESCAPED, WHAT MAGIC HE HAD
USED. HE SAID, ’IT IS A MATTER OF DESIGN, AND DESIGN WITHIN DESIGN. MY WIFE IS A
WEAVER. SHE FOUND THE MAN WHO HAD MADE THE LOCKS OF THE CELL DOOR, AND
GOT THE DESIGN FROM HIM. THIS SHE WOVE INTO THE CARPET AT THE SPOT WHERE MY
HEAD TOUCHED IN PRAYER FIVE TIMES A DAY. I AM A METAL-WORKER, AND THIS DESIGN
LOOKED TO ME LIKE THE INSIDE OF A LOCK. I DESIGNED THE PLAN OF THE ARTEFACTS
TO OBTAIN THE MATERIALS TO MAKE THE KEY – AND I ESCAPED.’
’THAT,’ SAID THE NAQSHBANDI SUFI, ’IS ONE OF THE WAYS IN WHICH MAN MAY MAKE HIS
ESCAPE FROM THE TYRANNY OF HIS CAPTIVITY.’

Keep weaving the carpet, we are working on the key......

### caroline — 2014-06-22T02:38:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131693>

As always Josh - thankyou!

I was just talking to Stella about all this ..... we seemed to have turned into a butterfly at the exact same time...how is that possible? Two people that had never known each other ...became instantly and forever entangled.

Now I understand why my old friends don't fit anymore ..... I make them slightly uncomfortable - I am moving forward at a record pace ...and they are stuck in comfortable ....the hard part is - I can't be around them - l have no connection there anymore ...they have no interest in moving forward and I have no interest in going backward!

I learn so much here every single day ..... I have a hard time with he QED .......but I sure do get - and love all this "Life" stuff. It is a magnificent combination!

I listened last nite to the May 2014 Q&A for the first time .... I can't even imagine, Dr. K. if you had needed to shut all this down ......and I certainly do understand what a toll this must take on you ....

We are always doing the asking ..... you also need to ask us for what you need too...... as you have done in this thread.

I am so glad that you have such a loving and supportive wife and family .....that is everything in my opinion.
but - you deserve nothing less .......you definitely reap what you sew ......

### kyrakitty — 2014-06-22T21:58:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131796>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=131743)
>
> this place has reignited my motivation to practice osteopathy and functional medicine, after a period of feeling disheartened.

Jack said: Here is a foundational issue about wellness.........the truth never changes, but you must.​

I tell the above to my clients (in my own words but same message) who ask me for advice on how to get better. They then proceed to not follow through and then demand answers to questions they keep asking over and over again. I tell them again the above and then I add the following (in my own words but same message):

Jack said: You have an obligation to yourself only. You can only change yourself. You can’t let other people tell you who you are. You have to decide that for yourself. Health improvements always begin with I, not we.​

This dance with my clients gets old real fast...being a health care practitioner is challenging especially when many clients/patients are like talking to brick walls sometimes what you say penetrates and other times it bounces right off due to not enough electrons... sometimes health care practitioners forget to take care of themselves first and then helping becomes a nuisance for them.

I see my clients doing the following clear as day:

Jack said: When you are sick you create a new identity and reality and you fall into it.​

But my clients don't see they are doing this... They taught me to constantly ask myself am I doing this too?

As a practitioner I am focusing more and more on taking care of myself first and letting my clients take responsibility for their choices. In the end I see that many of my clients will never change THEY ARE TOO COMFORTABLE WHERE THEY ARE AT.... AND WHAT THEY GET OUT OF BEING SICK IS MORE VALUABLE TO THEM THEN BEING HEALTHY.

In short they don't want to get better.. and I am learning to accept their choice to stay sick.

### ATL_Paleo — 2014-06-22T22:18:36+0300

Welcome to the US healthcare model. Their sessions with you give short term relief of their symptoms .... but they refuse to search (or accept) the root cause.

Their lack of true action should mean continued repeat business for you. Rinse, repeat. Cash their checks, take care of yourself, and say "Next".

They then proceed to not follow through and then demand answers to questions they keep asking over and over again. I tell them again the above and then I add the following (in my own words but same message):
>
>
> Jack said: You have an obligation to yourself only. You can only change yourself. You can’t let other people tell you who you are. You have to decide that for yourself. Health improvements always begin with I, not we.​
>
>
> This dance with my clients gets old real fast...being a health care practitioner is challenging especially when many clients/patients are like talking to brick walls sometimes what you say penetrates and other times it bounces right off due to not enough electrons... sometimes health care practitioners forget to take care of themselves first and then helping becomes a nuisance for them.
>
> I see my clients doing the following clear as day:
>
>
> Jack said: When you are sick you create a new identity and reality and you fall into it.​
>

> But my clients don't see they are doing this... They taught me to constantly ask myself am I doing this too?
>
> As a practitioner I am focusing more and more on taking care of myself first and letting my clients take responsibility for their choices. In the end I see that many of my clients will never change THEY ARE TOO COMFORTABLE WHERE THEY ARE AT.... AND WHAT THEY GET OUT OF BEING SICK IS MORE VALUABLE TO THEM THEN BEING HEALTHY.
>
> In short they don't want to get better.. and I am learning to accept their choice to stay sick.

ive said it many times, the best practitioner is the one who is the living embodiment of what they preach.
when the wheels fall off a patient, and you are still standing there living optimally, the truth eventually clicks for them.
i had a patient well up in tears as i discussed everything that has gone awry, as she realised that her beliefs subjugated her health. but she felt empowered to change because the spotlight was shone on the path to a new reality.
very true re: patient identity. people get tied to their labels and their story. "i have fibromyalgia" etc
i used to speak at a local fibromyalgia "support group" but really all it ever was, was a moan and groan group where they all wallowed in their story.
the first thing i remember saying was that their condition has a title, but that title does little to help them, so lets let go of the "named disease" and look at the foundational conditions that led to it.
i remember starting with grains and brominated products and saying they needed to avoid them. the questions started "what about biscuits?" "what about wholegrain bread??" one woman said "THATS PRACTICALLY MY WHOLE DIET!?" - she was scared that she had to change it, yet it still didnt click why she has this condition?!

all we can do as practitioners is lay out how it is, and let them come to the party. in my experience the ones who heed your advice tell many others who are of a similar ilk, which results in a client base who are nice to work with.

### Jude — 2014-06-23T03:58:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131835>

I failed to impress this weekend as well......need some cool selling advice to suck them in!!
Questioned re looking really healthy and esp DH.....

BUT......need some cool ways to break into that cognitive haze that surrounds them!....at least they recognized we've done something really different and that 3yrs later we're still looking "good"!!!!

Trouble is combating all the stuff they've read and **want to discuss**...and why not ..but lack of timekinda spoils all the fun....so have to follow up with reading material for those who've requested it.

Have one whose dh had suddenly decided on becoming a vegan......Another whose mum has picked up when given coconut oil..another who actually had heard of Chris Kessler.....she was the most informed and made the most headway with....

Now I have to make good info choices to inform these folk...I'd just prefer to talk ...but not possible.....any recommendations ?

### ATL_Paleo — 2014-06-23T04:24:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131840>

I think it is really simple ..... a person must be ready to accept change ..... and over 95% of people we talk to are NOT ready. They are really content to wallow in their own "stuff".

Just think of the people who have made it to this website, participated for a little while and then left for good. If all the resources of Jack and his website could not help them see the light, then think of the massive challenge we face.

Josh is totally correct .... lead by example. Actions are much more powerful than words...... If and when they are ready, their teacher will appear.

### caroline — 2014-06-23T04:50:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131842>

BUT ... they know I don't eat cake ........and they aren't prepared to go there - not ever! To them - that is not living!

These are all very intelligent people ....... I can only be the best example of health and vitality that I can be....

but I feel like I don't want to be around them - at least for not very long ...altho I love them dearly.

Where would we all be if JK hadn't stuck it out with us?

### caroline — 2014-06-23T09:33:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131879>

Dr. K. has always said ..... when the student arrives - the teacher in me appears.

I think we all need to head those words ..... nobody wants stuffed rammed down their throats - I know I don't .... no matter how well intentioned.

we need to support people when they asked tho.

My dearest friend clearly wants what I have ....but she is so entrenched in her dogma.

She is finally understanding that fat is good ..... so I now have convinced her to buy full fat yogurt - that is a huge step for her. I helped her buy MCT oil to mix into it ...

Her DIL was just down from Queensland and my friend was shocked to see she has lost 40K...and you will never guess what? she is eating somewhat like me!!! high fat, medium protein etc. my friend kept saying that is how Audrey eats!

So - we need to be the change that others see ........

### Jack Kruse — 2014-06-24T04:41:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-131948>

Balance is critical once you re attain your redox........before you regain balance you must be unbalanced toward health to offset the unbalance that got you ill or where you currently are now. We all need to remember the major work of the world is not done by geniuses. It is done by regular Joe's, who maintain balance in their lives, but who have learned to work in an extraordinary manner consistently over their lives. Life rises out of death, death rises out of life; in being opposite they yearn to each other, they give birth to each other and are forever reborn. Life is a balanced system of learning which drives evolution to eventually manifest wisdom. That wisdom is built into compliant or intelligent design of life's systems we call cells and tissues. Whether it be pleasure or pain; every situation in our life serves a purpose whether we realize it or not........It is up to us to recognize what that purpose might be.

### lavendercat — 2014-07-24T03:47:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-135360>

well, ive only been here a few days but im very happy with the site. i think the only way you could improve it would be if you would send me free oysters through the post which you have personally hand picked from the sea bed.

i think the way you interact with people here is exeptional and so rare. the fact that you want to improve on it even more speaks volumes. i would be quite happy just reading the blogs and applying things to my life, but the fact that you are addressing people individually in respect of their health and also in discussion i think is quite unbelievable really.

i cant think of anyone else who does this. maybe im out of touch and it happens more nowadays, but i think back to when i was a fan of susan powter and her occasional "i will now address my public and also you can all fuck off" videos. so many people who talk about nutrition do so in that condescending "i have spoken" kind of way. but this is more like a genuine invitation to partake in the curiosity about knowledge.

i dont know if any of you have come across Markus Rothkranz. well in my opinion he is completely ridiculous. that doesnt really relate to what im saying about this website but i wanted to say it anyway  if only for comparison about how to address people when it comes to their health.

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-23T15:01:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-more-help-to-help-you-better.10616/post-153691>

> [Albert83BCN said:](/goto/post?id=123060)
>
> I also loved the date index. In fact, I thought that you decided to get rid of it for some reason but now that I see your frustration, I wonder... why did it need to be removed? A technical issue? I can see that all posts still maintain their original date so... why not to rebuild a new index? Archives are sometimes a feature given with certain themes and not on others, on Wordpress. But, if your theme doesn't have it, there are plugins that allow to have an archive section, so it should be easy to get a new one. Here's some random posts I found on a quick search on the net with some info for you to digg in, in case you want to. I think your technical team shouldn't have much of a problem implementing something like this:
>
> [https://www.iacquire.com/blog/13-wordpress-plugins-to-organize-and-sort-your-content](https://www.iacquire.com/blog/13-wordpress-plugins-to-organize-and-sort-your-content)
> [http://www.wpuniversity.com/wordpress-tips/the-top-5-plugins-manage-your-pages-and-post-order/](http://www.wpuniversity.com/wordpress-tips/the-top-5-plugins-manage-your-pages-and-post-order/)
> [http://wordpress.org/plugins/smart-archives-reloaded/screenshots/](http://wordpress.org/plugins/smart-archives-reloaded/screenshots/)

Just making my way through this thread while trying to decide if I will buy a membership, and if so, which one. I saw this above post. I have no idea how things have continued to evolve here but I have still never found a blog index by date. Is there some reason you don't just hand-code one, and use a portal for the index page of the site (by which I mean, the landing page for people who come to jackkruse.com)?

My first degree was in internet programming. This is such a simple thing to do it baffles me that it ever caused such issue here. I'm sure it's been dealt with by now but just wanted to comment while I'm reading this - hand coding isn't hard. It's certainly easier than trying to figure out "apps" these days IMO. I could probably throw something together and have it on the server and functional by this time tomorrow if I actually tried.

Also wondering, has the issue been resolved yet with people not having 100% of their paid-for content available 100% of the time? If I'm going to consider buying a membership then I want to know ahead of time that I will never have to email someone to give me what I have already paid for. Thanks!
